name: Aider

form_factor.comment: CLI terminal app. Per-form-factor links: CLI: https://aider.chat/docs/install.html.

released_in.comment: First public release v0.5.0 on June 7, 2023.

latest_major_update.comment: Latest tagged release v0.86.0 (Aug 9, 2025). Main branch is actively developed beyond that.

rules.comment: Supports CONVENTIONS.md loaded via --read or .aider.conf.yml. No equivalent of a persistent auto-loaded CLAUDE.md file. Location: `.aider.conf.yml`, `.aider.model.settings.yml`, `.aider.model.metadata.json`, and repo convention files; no AGENTS.md standard.

skills.comment: Has /save and /load commands to persist/replay sequences of slash commands. Not a full reusable skill system like Cursor skills. Location: no first-class skill folder; use repo files/prompts/conventions.

hooks.comment: Only git pre-commit hook bypass via --git-commit-verify. No lifecycle hooks (session-start, session-end, etc.).

mcp_servers.comment: No MCP (Model Context Protocol) support detected in documentation, codebase, or feature listings. Location: no MCP config for Aider.

custom_commands.comment: 40+ built-in slash commands but no mechanism for users to define custom slash commands. Location: no custom command folder; uses CLI flags/config.

goal_command.comment: In-chat `/` commands (e.g. `/add`, `/commit`) are built-in chat commands, not a persisted `/goal` loop with pause/resume until a completion condition.

subagents.comment: Architect/Editor mode uses two models but no general subagent delegation system. No spawning of independent worker agents.

monitor.comment: No first-class background monitor/watch primitive found in reviewed docs. Aider has non-interactive one-shot execution and optional commands like --test/--lint, but no Monitor-equivalent for waiting on spawned jobs or event streams.

model_selection.comment: Full model selection via --model, --weak-model, --editor-model. In-chat /model command. Model aliases supported.

approval_mode.comment: Has --yes-always to auto-accept all confirmations. No granular approval mode like Cursor's always/accept-tool/plan-then-act.

sandbox_mode.comment: No filesystem or network sandbox. Has --dry-run flag that prevents file modification. Aider edits files directly in the working git repo.

resume.comment: No --resume flag. Has --restore-chat-history which restores previous chat messages but does not resume session state.

continue.comment: No --continue flag for auto-resuming the latest session. --restore-chat-history restores message history but is manual.

non_interactive.comment: Full non-interactive mode via --message/-m (send instruction, edit files, exit). Also --apply, --commit, --lint, --test.

output_format.comment: No structured machine-readable output format (JSON, etc.). Has --no-pretty for plain text, --verbose for debug info.

statusline.comment: No configurable status line. Has extensive color customization but no status bar/line concept.

telemetry.comment: Opt-in anonymous analytics via PostHog. Permanently disable with --analytics-disable. Never collects code/chat/keys.

custom_model_provider.comment: Connects to any LLM via litellm. Supports OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, DeepSeek, Ollama, OpenRouter, any OpenAI-compatible endpoint, and more.

notes: Aider is a mature open-source CLI coding agent (45k+ stars) since June 2023. Deep git integration, repo mapping for codebase context, extensive LLM provider support through litellm. No published embed SDK (CLI/scripting only). Notable gaps vs newer agents: no MCP, no user-defined slash commands, no sandbox, no subagent delegation, no resume/continue flags, no structured output. Apache-2.0 licensed.

pricing.comment: Free and open source under Apache 2.0. Users pay only for LLM API usage through their own API keys.

some_free_usage.comment: Aider documents free API providers and local-model options, so some agent usage can be free depending on provider/model choice.

no_account_required.comment: Can run against local Ollama models without signing into an Aider-hosted account.

can_use_other_subscriptions.comment: Can use external provider accounts/API keys and local OpenAI-compatible endpoints instead of an Aider-owned subscription.

hosted_agent.comment: Runs entirely locally via pip. No hosted agent platform.

transcripts.comment: Location: optional chat history/transcript files are configured with Aider history settings such as `.aider.chat.history.md`.

keyboard_shortcuts.comment: Limited keybinding support; primarily CLI-based with config options.

theme_customization.comment: CLI-based; no theme customization available.

auto_save.comment: Auto-save on changes; can be configured via command-line.

debugging_output.comment: Verbose mode available; debug output configurable.

context_window.comment: Max token limit and context window configurable.

code_formatting.comment: Auto-format support via prettier, black, and other formatters.

git_integration.comment: Git-aware workflow; commit generation, diff awareness.

performance_mode.comment: Memory and token limit controls.

ui_customization.comment: CLI-based; no UI customization.

name: Amazon Q Developer CLI

form_factor.comment: Standalone terminal CLI (`q chat`). Amazon Q Developer also ships as IDE extensions/plugins, but those are a separate surface not covered by this entry. Per-form-factor links: CLI: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/amazonq/latest/qdeveloper-ug/command-line-installing.html.

released_in.comment: Enhanced agentic `q chat` CLI experience (multi-turn, tool use, Claude 3.7 Sonnet reasoning) announced March 6, 2025. Earlier non-agentic CLI completions predate this, but agentic chat is the surface tracked here.

latest_major_update.comment: v1.19.7 (Nov 17, 2025) is the last tagged release before the project transitioned to the closed-source Kiro CLI; AWS has since announced Amazon Q Developer end-of-support with migration to Kiro CLI through 2026-2027.

rules.comment: Markdown rule files in `.amazonq/rules/` are auto-scanned and applied per project. No AGENTS.md support (open feature request, GitHub issue #2712, unimplemented). Location: `.amazonq/rules/*.md`, referenced via agent config `resources`.

skills.comment: Custom agent JSON configs (prompt, tools, resources, hooks) act as reusable, switchable capability profiles, but there is no dedicated 'skills' folder/format distinct from agent configs. Location: agent config files, typically under `.amazonq/cli-agents/` or `~/.aws/amazonq/cli-agents/`.

hooks.comment: Agent config `hooks` field supports agentSpawn, userPromptSubmit, preToolUse, postToolUse, and stop lifecycle hooks that run shell commands and inject output into context.

mcp_servers.comment: Supports both local (stdio) and remote (HTTP/OAuth) MCP servers via global `~/.aws/amazonq/mcp.json` and workspace `.amazonq/mcp.json`, merged with workspace taking precedence on conflicts.

custom_commands.comment: No first-class user-defined slash command system; closest equivalent is agent-config hooks/prompts and `/agent create` to switch agent profiles with different tool/resource sets.

goal_command.comment: No built-in `/goal`-style persistent-objective auto-continue loop; sessions proceed turn-by-turn or via `--no-interactive` single-shot prompts.

subagents.comment: Custom agents are independent, user-selected configuration profiles (one active per session); there is no delegation/spawning of subagents from within a running session.

monitor.comment: No Monitor-equivalent found in reviewed CLI docs. Amazon Q Developer CLI supports lifecycle hooks and custom agent profiles, but not a documented background command/subagent monitor that wakes the session on completion or output.

transcripts.comment: Conversation history is auto-saved per working directory to support `--resume`, but there is no documented user-facing export/transcript file format akin to a JSONL log.

model_selection.comment: Switch models in-session with `/model`, at launch with `q chat --model <name>`, or set a default via `q settings chat.defaultModel <name>`. Choices are limited to AWS-hosted Claude models (3.5/3.7 Sonnet, Sonnet 4, Sonnet 4.5, and an 'auto' option) served over Bedrock.

approval_mode.comment: Per-tool trust model: only `fs_read` is trusted by default; `/tools trust`, `/tools trust-all`, and CLI flags `--trust-all-tools`/`--trust-tools=<list>` control auto-approval, with `/tools reset` to restore defaults.

sandbox_mode.comment: No filesystem/network sandbox; security guidance instead recommends running in a dedicated environment and using tool-trust controls (`/tools untrust`) to gate risky actions like `use_aws` or `execute_bash`.

resume.comment: `q chat --resume` automatically picks up the most recent conversation saved for the current working directory.

continue.comment: `--resume` continues the latest session for the current directory without needing an explicit session ID, satisfying both resume and continue behavior.

non_interactive.comment: `q chat --no-interactive "<prompt>"` runs a single headless turn and exits; commonly paired with `--trust-all-tools` to avoid approval prompts in scripts/CI. Some early versions had bugs around prompts still appearing or trusted-tools lists being ignored (GitHub issues #1951, #1995), later patched (e.g. v2.0.1 fix notes).

output_format.comment: `q chat` only emits plain text/Markdown even in `--no-interactive` mode; structured `-f json|json-pretty` output exists only for other subcommands like `q settings`/`q whoami`, not for chat. A JSON/stream-JSON chat output format is an open, unimplemented feature request (issue #2713).

statusline.comment: No user-configurable status line; `q settings` covers behavior toggles (telemetry, default model) rather than a persistent status bar.

telemetry.comment: Telemetry/data-sharing is user-configurable via `q settings telemetry.enabled true|false`; Free tier content may be used for service improvement, Pro tier content is excluded by policy.

keyboard_shortcuts.comment: A handful of fixed shortcuts exist (Ctrl+D to exit, Ctrl+S for slash-command fuzzy search) but there is no user-customizable keybinding configuration.

theme_customization.comment: No documented theme/color customization for the terminal chat UI.

auto_save.comment: Conversations are automatically saved per working directory without user action, enabling `--resume`.

debugging_output.comment: CLI reference documents a `--verbose` style flag and `/tools` introspection, but there is no dedicated structured debug/trace log format.

context_window.comment: `/usage` shows estimated context window consumption and `/compact` summarizes history to free space, but the window size itself is fixed by the selected model, not user-configurable.

code_formatting.comment: No built-in formatter/linter integration; any formatting happens only if the agent invokes external tools (e.g. via `execute_bash`) itself.

git_integration.comment: No dedicated git tool/UI; git operations happen through the general `execute_bash` tool like any other shell command, subject to the same trust/approval rules.

performance_mode.comment: No documented caching/performance-tuning controls exposed to users beyond model choice and context compaction.

ui_customization.comment: Single-pane terminal chat UI; no panel/layout customization since this is a CLI, not an IDE surface.

hosted_agent.comment: Runs as a local terminal process talking to the AWS-hosted backend for inference; there is no separate hosted/cloud agent execution platform for this CLI surface.

custom_model_provider.comment: Model choice is restricted to a fixed list of Claude models served via AWS's own Amazon Bedrock-backed Q Developer service; no support for arbitrary/OpenAI-compatible model endpoints or bring-your-own-API-key providers.

notes: Amazon Q Developer CLI (`q chat`) is AWS's Rust-based, Bedrock-backed agentic terminal coding agent, launched as an enhanced agentic experience in March 2025. It requires AWS Builder ID or IAM Identity Center sign-in (no API-key-only or account-free path), supports MCP, lifecycle hooks, and per-project `.amazonq/rules/` markdown rules, but lacks AGENTS.md support, subagent delegation, sandboxing, and structured JSON chat output. As of mid-2026 the open-source repo is no longer actively maintained: AWS is sunsetting Amazon Q Developer in favor of the backward-compatible, closed-source Kiro CLI (same `q`/`q chat` entry points, configs auto-migrated to `~/.kiro/`), with new signups blocked from May 2026 and full IDE/subscription end-of-support by April 2027.

pricing.comment: Perpetual Free Tier (Builder ID or IAM) with monthly agentic-request limits; Pro tier is $19/user/month with higher limits and enterprise admin controls. New Free Tier/Pro signups are blocked starting May 15, 2026 as part of the Kiro CLI migration.

some_free_usage.comment: Free Tier includes a documented monthly allotment of agentic chat requests and code suggestions at no cost via AWS Builder ID.

no_account_required.comment: Requires signing in with an AWS Builder ID or IAM Identity Center/IAM credentials before any chat session; there is no local-model or account-free path.

can_use_other_subscriptions.comment: Models are served exclusively through AWS's own Amazon Bedrock-backed Q Developer service (Builder ID/IAM Identity Center auth); no option to route usage through a personal Anthropic/OpenAI API key or other vendor subscription.

name: Amp

form_factor.comment: CLI TUI + IDE extensions (VS Code, JetBrains, Neovim, Zed) + @ampcode/sdk (TypeScript) and amp-sdk (Python). June 2026 UI adds web and mobile surfaces for watching/driving agents. Per-form-factor links: CLI: https://ampcode.com/manual#getting-started; Extension: https://ampcode.com/manual/vscode; SDK: https://ampcode.com/manual/sdk; Web: https://ampcode.com/news/agents-everywhere; Mobile: https://ampcode.com/news/agents-everywhere.

released_in.comment: Publicly launched May 15, 2025 (no waitlist). Originally built inside Sourcegraph, spun off as Amp Frontier Corporation.

latest_major_update.comment: June 19, 2026 - Amp added custom agents via plugins, usable as the main agent, as subagents, in amp -x tool pipelines, or as many parallel worker agents.

rules.comment: Full support via AGENTS.md files (plural). Scanned from cwd, parent dirs, subtrees, $HOME/.config/amp/, /etc/ampcode/. Supports @-mentions, globs, YAML frontmatter. Location: no public disk folder/file location found in docs reviewed.

skills.comment: Full support. Skills are directories with SKILL.md + YAML frontmatter. Locations: .agents/skills/ (project), ~/.config/agents/skills/ (user). Can bundle MCP servers via mcp.json. Location: no public disk folder/file location found in docs reviewed.

hooks.comment: Full support via Plugin API (TypeScript). Events: session.start, agent.start, tool.call, tool.result, agent.end. Plugins can respond with allow/reject/modify/synthesize actions.

mcp_servers.comment: Full support. Add via CLI (amp mcp add) or config. Local (command/args/env) and remote (url/headers) servers. OAuth auto-flow. Skill bundling for lazy loading. Location: no public disk folder/file location found in docs reviewed.

custom_commands.comment: Removed January 29, 2026. Replaced by skills and command palette (Ctrl+O) instead of slash commands. Location: not applicable; feature not supported.

goal_command.comment: Amp documents CLI and editor workflows but no built-in `/goal` slash command for persistent session objectives.

subagents.comment: Full support. Spawned via Task tool. Built-in specialized subagents: Oracle (GPT-5.4), Librarian (code search), Painter (image gen), Review, Search.

monitor.comment: Task/subagent execution and stream-json/headless operation cover some wait-for-result workflows, but no dedicated background command/log/WebSocket monitor primitive was found in the manual.

model_selection.comment: Pre-set modes (smart=Claude Opus 4.7, deep=GPT-5.5 reasoning, rush=GPT-5.5 no-reasoning, free=mixed). You choose the mode, not the exact model.

approval_mode.comment: Default: no approval (dangerously-allow-all is now default). Custom approval policies possible via Plugin API (tool.call event).

sandbox_mode.comment: No built-in sandbox. Documentation suggests external isolated dev environments for untrusted code.

resume.comment: Full support. 'amp threads continue [thread id]' documented. Thread referencing (@T-xxx or URL) across sessions.

continue.comment: Full support. /continue command in CLI to pick old threads. Threads persist at ampcode.com/threads.

non_interactive.comment: Full support. amp --execute / amp -x for headless mode. Set AMP_API_KEY env var for CI/CD. Auto-detects piped stdout.

output_format.comment: Full support. --stream-json flag with --execute for Claude Code-compatible NDJSON streaming output. Multiple schema types.

statusline.comment: Not supported. Custom themes removed in the May 6, 2026 rebuild. No statusline customization.

telemetry.comment: No explicit telemetry settings found in documentation. No on/off telemetry toggle mentioned in manual or settings.

custom_model_provider.comment: No BYOM for the main agent (picks models per mode from providers). MCP servers can connect to external tools, and Plugin API allows custom tool registration.

notes: Amp is opinionated and aggressively removes features it considers obsolete (custom commands, slash commands, themes). Focuses on frontier models. Plugin API provides extensive extensibility for those who need it. Closed-source product; some auxiliary tools open source on GitHub.

pricing.comment: Usage-based: pay-as-you-go with zero markup on API costs for individuals. Minimum $5 credit purchase. Free mode (ad-supported, no longer shows ads). Enterprise: 50% markup + $1,000 one-time fee.

some_free_usage.comment: Amp documents a free mode for interactive editor/CLI use, with limits and availability caveats.

hosted_agent.comment: CLI runs locally. Threads shared via web but no managed agent platform.

transcripts.comment: Location: transcript/session disk storage was not found in public docs reviewed.

keyboard_shortcuts.comment: Research needed. Check official documentation for keybinding customization options.

theme_customization.comment: Research needed. Check for theme/appearance customization options.

auto_save.comment: Research needed. Check for auto-save or session persistence features.

debugging_output.comment: Research needed. Check for debug mode or verbose logging options.

context_window.comment: Research needed. Check for configurable context length or token limits.

code_formatting.comment: Research needed. Check for formatter/linter integration or auto-format options.

git_integration.comment: Research needed. Check for git command support or version control integration.

performance_mode.comment: Research needed. Check for caching, optimization, or resource management settings.

ui_customization.comment: Research needed. Check for UI layout, sidebar, or panel customization.

name: Antigravity

form_factor.comment: Google Antigravity IDE plus standalone agy CLI (plugin layout, rules, skills, hooks, MCP). Per-form-factor links: CLI: https://antigravity.google/docs/cli-getting-started; IDE: https://antigravity.google/download.

released_in.comment: IDE public release in 2025; standalone agy CLI documented May 2026.

latest_major_update.comment: CLI plugin system and IDE agent surfaces updated together in current docs.

rules.comment: Full plugin rules on CLI; IDE has persistent agent context with overlapping rules support. Location: Antigravity plugin rules live in plugin folders; workspace/global IDE rule storage is managed by Antigravity.

skills.comment: Plugin skills on CLI; IDE local data includes skills with less public doc detail. Location: plugin skill folders containing `SKILL.md`.

hooks.comment: Plugin hooks documented on CLI.

mcp_servers.comment: CLI plugin mcp_config.json; IDE also ships MCP configuration. Location: plugin `mcp_config.json` files and IDE/CLI MCP configuration.

custom_commands.comment: CLI via plugin capabilities; IDE via global workflows rather than classic slash-command folders. Location: command-like behavior is via plugins/workflows rather than a documented standalone command folder.

goal_command.comment: Antigravity CLI docs cover plugins, sandbox, and slash commands but do not document a Codex-style `/goal` primitive.

monitor.comment: Public Antigravity CLI/plugin docs reviewed here do not establish whether an agent-side background monitor/watch primitive exists.

model_selection.comment: IDE model UI exists; agy --help does not expose a model flag as of this dataset.

approval_mode.comment: CLI supports --dangerously-skip-permissions; IDE approval behavior not fully documented.

sandbox_mode.comment: CLI supports --sandbox for terminal restrictions.

resume.comment: CLI --continue/--conversation; IDE stores conversation/brain state.

continue.comment: CLI --continue for latest conversation; IDE continues from existing history.

non_interactive.comment: CLI supports --print / --prompt; IDE is interactive-first.

custom_model_provider.comment: No arbitrary provider configuration found in public CLI help/docs.

notes: Google Antigravity spans an agentic IDE and the agy CLI. Distinct from deprecated Gemini CLI. No published programmatic SDK.

pricing.value: Free (generous weekly rate limits), Google AI Pro/Ultra (undisclosed), Organization (consumption-based via Google Cloud)
pricing.comment: Free tier includes Gemini 3.5 Flash, 3.1 Pro, 3 Flash, Claude Sonnet & Opus 4.6, gpt-oss-120b. Pro/Ultra tiers offer more generous rate limits.

some_free_usage.comment: Google announced Antigravity public preview availability at no charge with rate limits.

hosted_agent.comment: Local IDE and CLI surfaces.

transcripts.comment: Location: Antigravity brain/trajectory summaries are under `~/.gemini/antigravity/brain/<uuid>/*.md`; index data is in `~/Library/Application Support/Antigravity/User/globalStorage/state.vscdb`.

keyboard_shortcuts.comment: Research needed. Check official documentation for keybinding customization options.

theme_customization.comment: Research needed. Check for theme/appearance customization options.

auto_save.comment: Research needed. Check for auto-save or session persistence features.

debugging_output.comment: Research needed. Check for debug mode or verbose logging options.

context_window.comment: Research needed. Check for configurable context length or token limits.

code_formatting.comment: Research needed. Check for formatter/linter integration or auto-format options.

git_integration.comment: Research needed. Check for git command support or version control integration.

performance_mode.comment: Research needed. Check for caching, optimization, or resource management settings.

ui_customization.comment: Research needed. Check for UI layout, sidebar, or panel customization.

form_factor.comment: CLI, IDE integration, VS Code extension, SDK. Per-form-factor links: CLI: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/quickstart; Extension: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/ide-integrations; SDK: https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/claude-code/sdk; Web: https://claude.ai/code; Mac App: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/desktop-quickstart.

released_in.comment: Claude Code was introduced as a limited research preview.

latest_major_update.comment: Latest release v2.1.198 (July 1, 2026) makes Claude in Chrome generally available, adds background-agent notifications, adds a /dataviz skill, improves background-agent PR handoff, lets Explore inherit the main session model, and fixes background-agent, plan-mode, network-retry, AWS gateway, rules, and UI issues.

rules.comment: Uses CLAUDE.md memory files. Location: `CLAUDE.md` in repo tree, plus `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` for user memory.

skills.comment: Supports skills. Location: `.claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md` or user-level `~/.claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md`.

hooks.comment: Supports lifecycle hooks.

mcp_servers.comment: Supports MCP servers. Location: project `.mcp.json` or Claude Code MCP config via `claude mcp`.

custom_commands.comment: Supports slash commands. Location: `.claude/commands/*.md` or `~/.claude/commands/*.md`.

goal_command.comment: Built-in `/goal` sets a completion condition; after each turn a fast evaluator decides whether Claude should continue until the condition holds (Claude Code v2.1.139+).

subagents.comment: Supports custom subagents.

monitor.comment: Dedicated Monitor tool runs a command in the background or connects to a WebSocket, feeds events back to Claude, and lets the same session continue until output/status changes arrive.

model_selection.comment: Model can be configured.

approval_mode.comment: Permission mode is configurable.

sandbox_mode.comment: Permissions and allowed tools provide sandbox-like controls.

resume.comment: Supports --resume.

continue.comment: Supports --continue for the most recent conversation.

non_interactive.comment: Supports --print.

output_format.comment: Supports output format controls.

statusline.comment: Supports custom status line.

telemetry.comment: Settings include telemetry and privacy controls.

custom_model_provider.comment: Supports Bedrock/Vertex and related integrations, not arbitrary providers in the same way as OpenAI-compatible CLIs.

pricing.value: Included in Pro $20/mo, Max 5x $100/mo, Max 20x $200/mo, Team $25-125/seat/mo, Enterprise $20/seat + API rates
pricing.comment: Claude Code is included in all subscription plans (Pro, Max, Team, Enterprise). Also available via API at standard token rates.

hosted_agent.comment: CLI runs locally; managed/cloud-based session execution is not directly exposed as a distinct platform.

transcripts.comment: Location: Claude Code CLI transcripts are JSONL under `~/.claude/projects/<proj-path-dashes>/<sessionId>.jsonl`.

keyboard_shortcuts.comment: Fully customizable keybindings via `.claude/keybindings.json` or settings UI.

theme_customization.comment: Dark/light mode and theme customization available in settings.

auto_save.comment: Auto-save behavior configurable in settings.

debugging_output.comment: Debug logs and diagnostic output available; verbosity configurable.

context_window.comment: Token limit and context window configurable per session.

code_formatting.comment: Integrates with workspace formatters; auto-format on save configurable.

git_integration.comment: Full git support: diff, commit, branch management built-in.

performance_mode.comment: Caching and optimization settings available; token budget management.

ui_customization.comment: Customizable sidebar, panel arrangement, collapsible sections.

name: Cline

form_factor.comment: VS Code extension, JetBrains plugin, CLI, and @cline/sdk for embedding the agent runtime in apps and CI. Per-form-factor links: Extension: https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=saoudrizwan.claude-dev; CLI: https://docs.cline.bot/cli/overview; SDK: https://docs.cline.bot/sdk/overview.

released_in.comment: Initial release was v1.0.4 on July 28, 2024 (originally named 'Claude Dev' by Saoud Rizwan). 8M+ installs by May 2026.

latest_major_update.comment: Latest release v4.0.5 (June 30, 2026) adds Claude Sonnet 5 support across Anthropic, Bedrock, Vertex, Claude Code, SAP AI Core, OpenRouter, and Vercel AI Gateway providers, including model-picker and recommended-model updates.

rules.comment: Rich rule system: .clinerules/ directory, global ~/Documents/Cline/Rules/, conditional rules with YAML frontmatter globs, auto-detection of .cursorrules, .windsurfrules, AGENTS.md. Location: no public disk folder/file location found in docs reviewed.

skills.comment: Full skill system: SKILL.md files in .cline/skills/ or ~/.cline/skills/, YAML frontmatter, progressive loading, slash command triggering, skill toggles. Location: no public disk folder/file location found in docs reviewed.

hooks.comment: Lifecycle hooks via SDK plugin system: 18 hook stages (input, runtime_event, session_start, turn_start, tool_call_before/after, etc.). Also ~/.cline/hooks/ directory.

mcp_servers.comment: Full MCP support with STDIO and HTTP/SSE transports, MCP Marketplace for one-click install, cline mcp CLI wizard. Location: no public disk folder/file location found in docs reviewed.

custom_commands.comment: Built-in slash commands plus skills auto-register as slash commands. SDK plugins can also register custom commands. Location: no public disk folder/file location found in docs reviewed.

goal_command.comment: Cline supports custom slash commands via workflow files; no built-in `/goal` for auto-continuing until a completion condition.

subagents.comment: Subagents are documented as enabled by default across VS Code, JetBrains, CLI, and other editors, with a Settings -> Features -> Agent toggle to disable the use_subagents tool.

monitor.comment: Cline exposes runtime/session/tool hooks and subagent task workflows, but reviewed docs do not show a dedicated command/log/WebSocket Monitor tool equivalent.

model_selection.comment: 30+ documented providers: Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, AWS Bedrock, Azure, GCP Vertex, OpenRouter (200+ models), Ollama, LM Studio, and any OpenAI-compatible endpoint.

approval_mode.comment: Granular auto-approve per category: Read, Edit, Execute, Browser, MCP. YOLO mode for everything. --auto-approve flag.

sandbox_mode.comment: Sandbox mode via CLINE_SANDBOX env var and CLINE_SANDBOX_DATA_DIR for isolated session storage. No containerization.

resume.comment: Full session resume via --id flag. cline history lists all sessions. Checkpoints enable rollback.

continue.comment: --continue flag exists but is tied to --worktree. No standalone 'continue latest' flag. Pipe-chaining supported.

non_interactive.comment: Full headless mode. Auto-triggered with --json flag, piped stdin, or redirected stdout. Works in CI/CD.

output_format.comment: Full JSON output via --json flag. Newline-delimited JSON objects with type, text, ts, say/ask subtype, reasoning, partial.

statusline.comment: No customizable status line found in documentation or CLI reference.

telemetry.comment: Anonymous telemetry with user toggle. Never captures code/file contents. Enterprise: OpenTelemetry integration (OTLP export to Datadog/Grafana/Splunk).

custom_model_provider.comment: Any OpenAI-compatible API endpoint supported. 30+ documented providers. BYOK model.

notes: Cline has the most comprehensive feature set of any coding agent in this matrix — 17/20 fields at full support. Open source (Apache 2.0), originally 'Claude Dev'. Started as VS Code extension, expanded to CLI and SDK. 8M+ installs.

pricing.comment: Open source: free for individual developers. Pay only for AI inference. Enterprise: custom pricing with SSO/OIDC/SCIM, RBAC.

some_free_usage.comment: Cline is free for individual developers; AI inference is paid separately or BYOK/local.

no_account_required.comment: Can run with local models via Ollama or LM Studio without a Cline-hosted account.

can_use_other_subscriptions.comment: Cline documents OpenAI Codex OAuth so users can bring a ChatGPT subscription, and also supports BYOK providers.

hosted_agent.comment: Runs locally as VS Code extension or CLI.

transcripts.comment: Location: transcript/session disk storage was not found in public docs reviewed.

keyboard_shortcuts.comment: VS Code extension keybindings; customizable via VS Code settings.

theme_customization.comment: Inherits VS Code theme; limited Cline-specific customization.

name: Crush

form_factor.comment: Terminal-first AI coding agent with a full-screen Bubble Tea TUI plus a `crush run` CLI/headless mode. Per-form-factor links: CLI/TUI install via package managers or `go install`.

released_in.comment: First tagged release v0.1.0, committed 2025-07-29. Repo created 2025-05-21.

latest_major_update.comment: Latest tagged release v0.81.0 (2026-06-29) is a UX-focused release adding a chat scrollbar and other interface/session improvements.

rules.comment: Auto-loaded global/project context files: `~/.config/crush/CRUSH.md` (Crush-specific) and `~/.config/AGENTS.md`/project `AGENTS.md` (generic, cross-tool standard). Path override via `global_context_paths`; supports folder recursion for `.md` files.

skills.comment: Supports the open 'Agent Skills' standard (SKILL.md packages). Discoverable from `$CRUSH_SKILLS_DIR`, `~/.config/agents/skills/`, and project `.agents/skills/`; invocable via the command palette with `user:`/`project:` prefixes; toggle via `disabled_skills`/`skills_paths` config.

hooks.comment: "Crush has preliminary support for hooks" per the README, deferring to a separate hook guide. Early/limited lifecycle hook support compared to mature hook systems.

mcp_servers.comment: Full MCP support across stdio, http, and sse transports, configured under `mcp` in crush.json. Supports shell-style value expansion ($VAR, ${VAR:-default}, $(command)), required-credential syntax (${VAR:?message}), per-server `disabled_tools`, and per-server timeouts.

custom_commands.comment: User-defined Markdown command files placed in `~/.config/crush/commands/` (or project-level), invoked from the command palette (press `/` or Ctrl+P, then Tab to list user commands). Public docs (COMMANDS.md) not yet merged to main as of mid-2026 but the feature is shipped.

goal_command.comment: No built-in `/goal` persistent-objective auto-continue loop. Sessions and `crush run --continue` support resuming work, but no dedicated goal-tracking/completion-condition command.

subagents.comment: Built-in "Coder" and "Task" agent types; the Coder agent can spawn restricted-permission Task subagents for delegated subtasks via an agent/task tool, with costs rolling up to the parent session. Agents configurable (model, allowed tools/MCP, context paths) via the `agents` config section.

monitor.comment: Crush can delegate to Task subagents and has preliminary hooks, but reviewed docs do not show a dedicated monitor/watch primitive for long-running commands or event streams.

transcripts.comment: Local SQLite-backed session storage with a dedicated `crush session` CLI subcommand: `list`, `show <id>`, `last`, `delete`, `rename`, each supporting `--json` for machine-readable transcripts including full message parts (text, reasoning, tool calls/results).

model_selection.comment: `-m/--model` and `--small-model` flags on `crush run`; in-TUI model switching mid-session while preserving context. Model database from Catwalk (community-supported open registry), auto-updated unless `disable_provider_auto_update`/`CRUSH_DISABLE_PROVIDER_AUTO_UPDATE=1` is set.

approval_mode.comment: Default mode prompts for permission before tool calls. `-y/--yolo` flag (or `permissions.yolo`/`SkipPermissionRequests`) auto-accepts all permissions. Granular `permissions.allowed_tools` allowlist for specific tools without prompting.

sandbox_mode.comment: No filesystem/network sandbox. Safety relies on permission prompts and the `--yolo` opt-out, plus `.crushignore` to exclude files from agent access.

resume.comment: `-s/--session <id>` resumes a specific prior session by full UUID, hash, or hash prefix; works both for the interactive TUI (`crush --session`) and `crush run --session`.

continue.comment: `-C/--continue` flag continues the most recently updated top-level session without needing an ID, for both `crush` (TUI) and `crush run` (non-interactive).

non_interactive.comment: `crush run "<prompt>"` (alias `r`) runs a single prompt non-interactively and exits; supports stdin piping, output redirection, `-q/--quiet` (hide spinner), `-v/--verbose` (show logs), and session continuation flags.

output_format.comment: `crush session list/show/last/delete/rename --json` give structured machine-readable output. `crush run` itself streams plain assistant text to stdout (no `--output-format json` for the run command).

statusline.comment: No user-configurable status line concept documented; the TUI shows fixed chrome (model, session, busy state) rather than a customizable status line.

telemetry.comment: Pseudonymous usage metrics tied to a device-specific hash; prompts/responses are never collected. Opt-out via `CRUSH_DISABLE_METRICS=1`, `disable_metrics: true` in config, or the `DO_NOT_TRACK=1` convention.

keyboard_shortcuts.comment: Built-in keybindings (e.g. `/` or Ctrl+P for command palette, Ctrl+O to open editor) exist and are documented in-app, but no general user-remappable keybindings config has been confirmed; some keybinding behavior is still being refined (open issues).

theme_customization.comment: Colors are auto-derived per model provider and dark/light terminal background detection (via charmtone/lipgloss); a TODO comment in config.go notes themes may be added later, but no user-selectable theme config exists yet. `transparent` TUI background toggle is supported.

auto_save.comment: Sessions/messages persist automatically to a local SQLite database under the data directory; no manual save action is required.

debugging_output.comment: Global `-d/--debug` flag and `debug`/`debug_lsp` config options; `crush run -v/--verbose` shows logs; `crush logs [--tail N] [--follow]` views/tails the log file at `./.crush/logs/crush.log`.

context_window.comment: No user-configurable context window/token-limit override documented; context window is determined by the selected model/provider via the Catwalk model database.

code_formatting.comment: No built-in formatter/linter integration documented beyond LSP-provided diagnostics; relies on LSP servers (gopls, typescript-language-server, etc.) for code intelligence rather than auto-formatting.

git_integration.comment: Respects `.gitignore` by default (plus `.crushignore` for extra exclusions); commit/PR message attribution customizable via `attribution.trailer_style` (assisted-by/co-authored-by/none) and `generated_with`. No dedicated diff-view UI or branch-management commands beyond what the agent does via shell tools.

performance_mode.comment: No documented caching/performance-tuning controls beyond standard provider model selection (large/small model split for cost/speed tradeoffs).

ui_customization.comment: TUI options include `compact_mode`, `diff_mode`, completion behavior, and `transparent` background toggle, but no panel/layout rearrangement or sidebar customization.

hosted_agent.comment: Runs locally as a terminal binary (optionally with a local client/server mode via `crush serve` for shared workspaces); no Charm-hosted cloud agent platform.

custom_model_provider.comment: Connects to Anthropic, OpenAI, Groq, OpenRouter, Vercel AI Gateway, Google Gemini, Synthetic, Z.ai, MiniMax, Hugging Face, Cerebras, io.net, Alibaba, Avian, Google Cloud Vertex AI, Amazon Bedrock, Azure OpenAI, and local models (Ollama, LM Studio, MLX, LiteLLM) via Catwalk's open model registry plus fully custom OpenAI-/Anthropic-compatible provider configs.

notes: Crush is Charm's open-source (FSL-1.1-MIT), terminal-first AI coding agent, first released July 2025 and under very active daily development (180+ tagged releases by mid-2026, 25k+ GitHub stars). It pairs a polished Bubble Tea TUI with a scriptable `crush run` headless mode, broad MCP/LSP/provider support via the community Catwalk model registry, and an emerging Agent Skills + custom-commands + Task-subagent ecosystem. Notable gaps versus more mature agents: no sandboxing, no user-selectable themes yet (TODO in source), and only preliminary lifecycle hooks.

pricing.comment: Free and source-available under the Functional Source License (converts to MIT after 2 years per version). Users pay only for their own LLM provider usage; some providers offer Crush-specific coding plans (GLM Coding Plan, Kimi Code, MiniMax Coding Plan).

some_free_usage.comment: Local model backends (Ollama, LM Studio, MLX, LiteLLM) and some free-tier provider plans allow free agent usage without paying for hosted inference.

no_account_required.comment: Can run entirely against local Ollama/LM Studio/MLX models without any Crush account or vendor sign-in; the CLI itself requires no account to install or run.

can_use_other_subscriptions.comment: Routes through the user's own API keys/accounts for any supported provider (Anthropic, OpenAI, etc.) or coding-plan subscriptions (GLM Coding Plan, Kimi Code, MiniMax Coding Plan, Synthetic) rather than a Crush-owned subscription.

name: Cursor

form_factor.comment: VS Code-derived AI IDE with standalone CLI (cursor-agent), @cursor/sdk (TypeScript/Python), and iOS mobile app for launching/managing cloud agents. Per-form-factor links: IDE: https://cursor.com/downloads; CLI: https://docs.cursor.com/en/cli/installation; SDK: https://cursor.com/docs/sdk/typescript; Mobile: https://cursor.com/changelog#cursor-mobile-app-for-ios.

released_in.comment: IDE launched ~2023; CLI surface added later (2025).

latest_major_update.comment: June 30, 2026 changelog expands Team Marketplaces with Team MCP servers distributed across cloud agents, the agents window, IDE, and CLI, plus organization-group marketplace access. June 29 also added the Cursor iOS public beta for launching and managing cloud agents from mobile.

rules.comment: Supports project and user rules. Location: `.cursor/rules/*.mdc` in a project; user/global rules are configured in Cursor settings.

skills.comment: Supports skills. Location: Cursor rules/agents features, not a SKILL.md folder standard in this row.

hooks.comment: Dedicated hooks surface: sessionStart/sessionEnd, preToolUse/postToolUse/postToolUseFailure, subagentStart/subagentStop, beforeShellExecution/afterShellExecution, beforeMCPExecution/afterMCPExecution, beforeReadFile/afterFileEdit, beforeSubmitPrompt, preCompact, stop, afterAgentResponse/afterAgentThought, Tab hooks, and workspaceOpen. Configured via .cursor/hooks.json at project, user, team, and enterprise levels; cloud agents run project/team/enterprise command-based hooks. Third-party Claude Code hooks are also supported.

mcp_servers.comment: Supports MCP servers in both IDE and CLI. Location: `.cursor/mcp.json` for project MCP servers or user-level Cursor MCP settings.

custom_commands.comment: CLI exposes full command controls; IDE has reusable rule/workflow patterns. Location: custom command support is through Cursor features/SDK rather than a repo command folder.

goal_command.comment: Cursor Agent CLI documents `/plan`, `/ask`, and `/resume` slash commands but no built-in `/goal` for persistent completion conditions.

subagents.comment: Full support in Cursor SDK as of June 2026: agents can nest subagents to any depth. IDE agent tasks remain available.

monitor.comment: Cursor supports hooks, cloud agents, and nested subagents, giving partial task/status monitoring, but reviewed docs do not show a dedicated command/log/WebSocket Monitor tool.

model_selection.comment: Supports model settings in IDE and CLI.

approval_mode.comment: Agent modes and CLI yolo/force controls expose approval controls.

sandbox_mode.comment: CLI exposes sandbox controls; IDE trust and agent controls provide partial sandboxing.

resume.comment: Composer/chat history in IDE and CLI resume/continue workflows support session resumption.

continue.comment: Supports --continue in CLI and conversation history in IDE.

non_interactive.comment: CLI supports print/headless mode; IDE is interactive only.

output_format.comment: CLI has partial structured output support; IDE has none.

statusline.comment: Editor status bar customization inherited from VS Code, not an agent-specific statusline.

telemetry.comment: Cursor documents privacy settings.

hosted_agent.comment: CLI agent can execute in Cursor's cloud runtime.

custom_model_provider.comment: Supports custom API keys/models in IDE and CLI.

notes: Cursor is a VS Code-derived AI IDE with a companion CLI (cursor-agent) for headless/CI use and @cursor/sdk for programmatic local/cloud agents.

pricing.comment: Individual: Pro $20/mo ($20 API usage), Pro+ $60/mo ($70 API usage), Ultra $200/mo ($400 API usage). Teams $40/user/mo.

some_free_usage.comment: Cursor pricing documents a Hobby Free plan with limited Agent requests and Tab completions.

transcripts.comment: Location: Cursor CLI transcripts live under `~/.cursor/projects/<slug>/agent-transcripts/<agentId>/<agentId>.jsonl`; Cursor IDE composer data is in SQLite `~/Library/Application Support/Cursor/User/globalStorage/state.vscdb`.

keyboard_shortcuts.comment: Full VS Code keybindings support; customizable via settings.

theme_customization.comment: Dark/light mode, custom themes, color schemes.

auto_save.comment: Auto-save configurable; interval/on-focus options.

debugging_output.comment: Debug console, verbose mode, diagnostic output available.

context_window.comment: Context length configurable for AI requests.

code_formatting.comment: Formatter integration, auto-format on save, linter support.

git_integration.comment: Full git integration: source control, diff, branch management.

performance_mode.comment: Performance settings, caching, resource management.

ui_customization.comment: Customizable layout, sidebar, panels (VS Code-based).

name: Devin

form_factor.comment: Web app + Devin Desktop/IDE plugin surfaces + CLI + Slack/Teams + REST API and official devin-sdk (Python). CLI installs the `devin` binary via Devin Desktop or the standalone CLI installer/Homebrew cask. Per-form-factor links: Extension: https://docs.devin.ai/desktop/command/plugins-overview; CLI: https://docs.devin.ai/cli; Web: https://app.devin.ai/; SDK: https://docs.devin.ai/api-reference/overview.

released_in.comment: First publicly demoed March 2024. Made Generally Available (GA) on December 10, 2024.

latest_major_update.comment: July 1, 2026 release notes add network access requests for restricted sessions, PR-link preferences, persisted default model selection, Slack bidirectional thread sync and bang-command improvements, MCP read-only mode, webhook setup improvements, skills analytics, ACU audit trails, redesigned environments, cross-platform repository cloning, and numerous review/analytics/UI fixes.

rules.comment: AGENTS.md files in repos, Knowledge (persistent org-level instructions with trigger descriptions), Skills & Rules settings. Location: hosted/web/API-managed; no local disk folder documented.

skills.comment: Full Agent Skills standard support. SKILL.md files in .agents/skills/. Supports YAML frontmatter, dynamic $ARGUMENTS and !command blocks. Also Playbooks system. Location: hosted/web/API-managed; no local disk folder documented.

hooks.comment: Automations engine: Slack/GitHub/Linear/schedule/cron/webhook triggers. Actions include start session, message session, triage. Payload filtering and secret auth.

mcp_servers.comment: MCP Marketplace with 40+ pre-built integrations. Supports STDIO, SSE, and HTTP transports. Custom MCP server tools for session/playbook/knowledge management. Location: hosted/web/API-managed; no local disk folder documented.

custom_commands.comment: Built-in slash commands (/plan, /review, /test, /think-hard, /implement) plus org-level custom slash commands. Also @skills:name syntax and macros. Location: hosted/web/API-managed; no local disk folder documented.

goal_command.comment: Devin supports team custom slash commands; no documented `/goal` for persistent thread-level objectives with auto-continuation.

subagents.comment: Yes, called 'Managed Devins'. Coordinator spawns child Devin sessions in isolated VMs for parallel work. Auto-triage also spawns sub-devins.

monitor.comment: Devin sessions and Managed Devins are cloud-hosted and inspectable, but reviewed docs do not expose a Monitor-style primitive for an agent to watch arbitrary command output or event streams.

model_selection.comment: Not explicitly user-facing. Devin offers 'Fast Mode' (~2x faster at 4x ACU). No documentation about letting users choose the underlying LLM model.

approval_mode.comment: 'Secure Mode' (disables native deployment, restricts outbound access). ACU caps, RBAC, PR review approval. No granular approval per action.

sandbox_mode.comment: Full isolation. Every session gets a fresh ephemeral snapshot. Enterprise VPC deployment option. VPN configuration supported.

resume.comment: API auto-resumes suspended sessions. CLI /resume alias. PR Resuming feature (Apr 2026). Archive/unarchive with undo.

continue.comment: CLI /handoff transfers to cloud Devin with full context (git branch + conversation history). API session messaging for continuation.

non_interactive.comment: Full headless/API mode. REST API with service user auth. Automations run fully autonomously (CI fixers, scheduled sessions, auto-triage).

output_format.comment: API returns structured JSON. Sessions support structured output schemas. No documented machine-readable mode for the CLI itself.

statusline.comment: No statusline feature found in any documentation. Web app shows session status indicators but no CLI statusline.

telemetry.comment: No user-configurable telemetry setting found in documentation. Enterprise audit logs available. Privacy controls mentioned but no opt-in/opt-out toggle.

custom_model_provider.comment: Cerebras mentioned as enterprise-ready inference provider. No self-serve BYO model option documented.

notes: Devin pioneered the autonomous AI software engineer category. Focuses on enterprise: VM isolation, managed sub-agents, automations. Higher-end pricing reflects the hosted, managed approach. Windsurf acquisition announced April 2026.

pricing.comment: Free (limited), Pro ($20/mo), Max ($200/mo), Teams ($80/mo minimum). Enterprise: custom pricing with VPC, SAML/OIDC. ACU-based consumption model.

some_free_usage.comment: Cognition announced a Free self-serve Devin plan with limited access.

hosted_agent.comment: Primarily cloud-hosted at app.devin.ai; Devin CLI bridges to cloud agent.

transcripts.comment: Location: hosted/web/API-managed session history; no local transcript directory documented.

name: GitHub Copilot CLI

form_factor.comment: Standalone terminal CLI, distinct from the GitHub Copilot cloud coding agent and IDE agent mode (see github-copilot-coding-agent entry). Installed via npm, Homebrew, WinGet, or install script; run as `copilot`. Per-form-factor links: CLI: https://docs.github.com/en/copilot/how-tos/copilot-cli/set-up-copilot-cli/install-copilot-cli.

released_in.comment: Public preview announced September 25, 2025, authenticated with existing GitHub account, usable with existing Copilot Pro/Pro+/Business/Enterprise plans.

latest_major_update.comment: Reached general availability for all Copilot subscribers on Feb 25, 2026, adding autopilot mode, /diff and /review commands, cross-session repo memory, and auto-compaction. Later 2026 updates added cloud/local sandboxes (--cloud, /sandbox enable), a unified /settings command, rubber duck critic agent, voice input, and prompt scheduling.

rules.comment: Reads AGENTS.md (repo root, cwd, or COPILOT_CUSTOM_INSTRUCTIONS_DIRS paths) and .github/instructions/**/*.instructions.md, plus CLAUDE.md/GEMINI.md at repo root, and a personal $HOME/.copilot/copilot-instructions.md. Disable with --no-custom-instructions.

skills.comment: Open Agent Skills standard: SKILL.md plus optional supplementary files/scripts, loaded when relevant to a task.

hooks.comment: sessionStart, sessionEnd, preToolUse (can deny/modify tool calls), postToolUse hooks, run synchronously and can block execution. Configured as JSON files at .github/hooks/*.json.

mcp_servers.comment: Ships with the GitHub MCP server enabled by default plus support for arbitrary custom MCP servers (stdio/http/sse). Flags: --additional-mcp-config, --disable-mcp-server, --disable-builtin-mcps, --add-github-mcp-tool(set), --enable-all-github-mcp-tools. /mcp slash command lists configured servers.

custom_commands.comment: Built-in slash commands (/resume, /cwd, /add-dir, /mcp, /diff, /review, /settings, /theme, /sandbox, /rewind) plus custom agents act as a structured-prompt mechanism, but no user-defined arbitrary slash command syntax is documented.

goal_command.comment: No `/goal` command, but --autopilot / --mode autopilot (Shift+Tab to cycle modes) auto-continues until the agent decides the task is complete, hits a blocking problem, Ctrl+C, or --max-autopilot-continues is reached. Scoped to a single task/session, not a persistent cross-session objective.

subagents.comment: Custom agents defined as Markdown agent-profile files (prompts, tools, MCP servers), invoked via --agent=NAME or in-session. Each spins up a subagent with its own isolated context window. Built-in specialized agents include Explore, Task, and the Rubber Duck critic agent.

monitor.comment: Copilot CLI supports hooks, custom agents, and cloud/headless execution, but reviewed docs do not show a dedicated Monitor-equivalent for waiting on arbitrary background command output or event feeds.

transcripts.comment: Local session logs under ~/.copilot/logs/ (configurable via --log-dir). Sessions can be exported with --share=PATH (Markdown file) or --share-gist (secret GitHub gist) after a programmatic run.

model_selection.comment: --model=MODEL flag or COPILOT_MODEL env var, plus in-session /model picker. Choices include Anthropic Claude, OpenAI GPT, and Google Gemini family models; `auto` lets Copilot pick automatically. BYOK external models also appear in the picker.

approval_mode.comment: Per-action approval prompts by default (Yes / Yes-and-approve-for-session / No-and-redirect). Granular flags: --allow-tool, --deny-tool, --allow-all-tools, --allow-all-paths, --allow-all-urls, --allow-url/--deny-url, --add-dir, plus --allow-all/--yolo to bypass everything. Enterprises can disable bypass via permissions.disableBypassPermissionsMode.

sandbox_mode.comment: Public preview (as of mid-2026): local sandbox via /sandbox enable restricts filesystem/network/system access (built on Microsoft MXC, included in standard seat); cloud sandbox via `copilot --cloud` runs a fully isolated, ephemeral GitHub-hosted Linux environment (Azure Container Apps Sandboxes) resumable from any device.

resume.comment: -r/--resume[=VALUE] picks from a session list or jumps to a session ID/prefix/name; /resume in-session. --connect[=SESSION-ID] attaches to a remote session; --remote enables resuming a remote task locally.

continue.comment: --continue resumes the most recent session in the current working directory, falling back to the globally most recent session, without needing a session ID.

non_interactive.comment: -p/--prompt=PROMPT executes a prompt programmatically and exits; --allow-all-tools is required for unattended runs. -i/--interactive=PROMPT starts interactive with an initial prompt. Documented for use in scripts and GitHub Actions workflows.

output_format.comment: --output-format=text|json (JSON outputs JSONL, one object per line). -s/--silent restricts output to just the agent response (no usage stats), useful when combined with -p for scripting.

statusline.comment: Experimental statusLine.command setting in ~/.copilot/settings.json runs a user script (receives session JSON over stdin) to render a custom status line; community tools (Oh My Posh integrations, copilot-statusline, copilot-hud) build on it. Marked experimental/subject to change as of mid-2026.

telemetry.comment: Enterprise/org-level audit logging and data-retention controls inherited from GitHub Copilot governance; --secret-env-vars redacts env vars from logs/MCP servers. No granular end-user telemetry opt-out UI documented specifically for the CLI.

keyboard_shortcuts.comment: Fixed shortcuts documented (Shift+Tab to cycle agent modes, Ctrl+C to stop autopilot, Ctrl+X then V or hold space for voice input); no user-remappable keybinding config found.

theme_customization.comment: /theme command and settings.json support auto/dark/light color modes only; no custom color palette/theme file support as of mid-2026 (feature requests open in github/copilot-cli issues).

auto_save.comment: Sessions are automatically persisted locally (and to the cloud when --remote/sandbox features are used) without an explicit save step, enabling --resume/--continue.

debugging_output.comment: --log-level=none|error|warning|info|debug|all|default and --log-dir=DIRECTORY (default ~/.copilot/logs/) for verbose/diagnostic logging.

context_window.comment: Automatic context auto-compaction and cross-session repository memory are documented; no explicit user-configurable context-length/token-limit setting found.

code_formatting.comment: No documented built-in formatter/linter integration toggle beyond what shell tool execution and repo tooling (e.g. pre-commit) provide.

git_integration.comment: /diff and /review commands for reviewing changes, GitHub.com integration for issues/PRs via natural language, an experimental git-free /rewind to restore only Copilot-changed files, and shell passthrough (prefix `!`) for arbitrary git commands.

performance_mode.comment: --effort/--reasoning-effort=low|medium|high|xhigh|max tunes reasoning depth vs. speed for supporting models; --enable-reasoning-summaries for OpenAI models. No general cache/perf toggle beyond this.

ui_customization.comment: --banner/--no-banner, --mouse/--no-mouse, --screen-reader, --plain-diff, --stream=MODE flags, plus an experimental redesigned terminal UI (/experimental) with tabs for Session/Issues/Pull requests/Gists. /settings unifies most config since June 2026.

hosted_agent.comment: Copilot CLI itself is a local terminal client. Cloud sandbox (`copilot --cloud`) runs the same CLI session in a GitHub-hosted ephemeral environment but is still user-initiated/interactive, not the autonomous GitHub Actions cloud agent (see separate github-copilot-coding-agent entry, hosted_agent=full).

custom_model_provider.comment: BYOK env vars (COPILOT_PROVIDER_TYPE, COPILOT_PROVIDER_BASE_URL, COPILOT_PROVIDER_API_KEY, COPILOT_MODEL) connect Anthropic, OpenAI-compatible endpoints, Azure OpenAI, AWS Bedrock, Google AI Studio, xAI/Microsoft Foundry, and local Ollama models.

notes: GitHub Copilot CLI is the standalone, local terminal coding agent (`copilot`, npm package @github/copilot, repo github/copilot-cli) — distinct from the asynchronous GitHub Actions-based Copilot cloud coding agent. Public preview Sep 2025, GA Feb 2026, runs on an existing Copilot subscription with no separate CLI fee. Strong open-standard support (AGENTS.md, Agent Skills, MCP, hooks, custom agents/subagents) and BYOK for external model providers, plus newer cloud/local sandboxing, autopilot mode, and an experimental redesigned terminal UI.

pricing.value: Included with Copilot Pro $10/mo, Pro+ $39/mo, Business $19/user/mo, Enterprise $39/user/mo; Free tier has limited usage
pricing.comment: Consumes GitHub AI Credits/premium requests against existing Copilot plan; usage-based billing for all plans started June 1, 2026, replacing the old premium-request counter. No CLI-specific surcharge.

some_free_usage.comment: GitHub Copilot Free plan includes limited chat/agent requests (e.g. 50 chat requests/mo) usable via the CLI, but no unlimited free local-model path is documented for Copilot CLI itself.

no_account_required.comment: Requires sign-in with a GitHub account holding an active Copilot subscription/seat; `/login` flow inside the CLI.

can_use_other_subscriptions.comment: BYOK support for Anthropic, OpenAI, Azure OpenAI, AWS Bedrock, Google AI Studio, xAI/Microsoft Foundry, any OpenAI-compatible endpoint, and local models via Ollama, configured with COPILOT_PROVIDER_TYPE / COPILOT_PROVIDER_BASE_URL / COPILOT_PROVIDER_API_KEY / COPILOT_MODEL env vars.

name: GitHub Copilot

form_factor.comment: IDE agent mode (VS Code, JetBrains, Xcode, Neovim, Eclipse) + CLI + hosted cloud agent + @github/copilot-sdk (public preview). On GitHub CLI installs, Copilot is launched as `gh copilot`, which downloads/runs the Copilot CLI payload. Per-form-factor links: IDE: https://docs.github.com/en/copilot/get-started/features; Extension: https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=GitHub.copilot; CLI: https://docs.github.com/en/copilot/how-tos/copilot-cli/use-copilot-cli/overview; SDK: https://docs.github.com/en/copilot/how-tos/copilot-sdk; Web: https://github.com/features/copilot.

released_in.comment: Announced at GitHub Universe (Oct/Nov 2024) in preview. GA billing launch: June 18, 2025 when premium request billing began.

latest_major_update.comment: Copilot desktop app (technical preview): parallel agent sessions, PR lifecycle, scheduled workflows. Third-party agent support added. Usage-based billing announced.

rules.comment: Multiple layers: .github/copilot-instructions.md (repo-wide), path-specific .github/instructions/**, AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, GEMINI.md, personal instructions, org-level. Location: no public disk folder/file location found in docs reviewed.

skills.comment: Open Agent Skills standard. .github/skills, .claude/skills, .agents/skills. Personal skills in ~/.copilot/skills. gh skill CLI for discovery/install. Location: no public disk folder/file location found in docs reviewed.

hooks.comment: Full lifecycle hook system: sessionStart, sessionEnd, userPromptSubmitted, preToolUse (approve/deny), postToolUse, agentStop, subagentStop, errorOccurred. .github/hooks/*.json.

mcp_servers.comment: Full MCP in cloud agent, CLI, and IDE. Supports local, stdio, http, sse transports. Built-in GitHub MCP server. Enterprise allowlist controls. Location: no public disk folder/file location found in docs reviewed.

custom_commands.comment: Built-in slash commands in IDE (/fix, /tests, /explain, /help) and CLI (/delegate, /pr, /resume). Prompt files (*.prompt.md) for reusable snippets. No user-defined custom slash commands. Location: no public disk folder/file location found in docs reviewed.

goal_command.comment: GitHub Copilot CLI has slash commands and agent modes but no `/goal` for persistent completion conditions across turns.

subagents.comment: Subagents in VS Code and JetBrains IDE agent mode. Isolated context window. Invocation: automatic, direct, or via #runSubagent. Cannot create sub-subagents.

monitor.comment: Copilot coding agent runs hosted jobs and exposes session/progress surfaces, but no Monitor-style command/log/WebSocket watcher for arbitrary agent-side events was found.

model_selection.comment: Models from Anthropic, Google, OpenAI, xAI, Microsoft, and GitHub-tuned models. Auto model selection is available; current billing uses token-based AI Credits by model, with legacy premium-request multipliers only for qualifying annual Pro/Pro+ subscribers.

approval_mode.comment: Desktop app has Interactive, Plan, and Autopilot modes. preToolUse hook can approve/deny. No universal 'always ask before X' toggle.

sandbox_mode.comment: Cloud agent runs in ephemeral GitHub Actions environment (natural sandbox). CLI has tool permission controls. No explicit sandbox toggle.

resume.comment: CLI: copilot --resume opens session picker, copilot --resume SESSION-ID jumps to specific session. /resume during interactive session.

continue.comment: CLI: copilot --continue resumes most recent session directly. IDE agent sessions persistent. Desktop app sessions in sidebar.

non_interactive.comment: CLI programmatic/scripted usage in GitHub Actions. --non-interactive mode. The GitHub CLI extension entrypoint is `gh copilot -- ...`; standalone payload availability can depend on the extension downloader/auth state. Copilot SDK for API-based integration.

output_format.comment: Copilot SDK provides programmatic access with structured responses. No documented --json flag for general CLI output.

statusline.comment: No statusline feature documented anywhere in GitHub Copilot's documentation.

telemetry.comment: Enterprise audit logging, agent session monitoring. Data retention: prompts retained 28 days. Users can opt out of training data. No granular telemetry UI.

custom_model_provider.comment: BYOK in CLI: env vars for provider type, base URL, API key. OpenAI-compatible endpoints, Azure OpenAI, Anthropic, local models via Ollama.

notes: GitHub Copilot's agent capabilities span IDE, cloud, and CLI. The cloud agent is unique: runs in ephemeral GitHub Actions environment. Desktop app (preview) adds parallel agent sessions. Ecosystem is rapidly evolving with weekly feature drops.

pricing.value: Free $0, Pro $10/mo, Pro+ $39/mo, Max $100/mo, Business $19/user/mo, Enterprise $39/user/mo
pricing.comment: Current plans use GitHub AI Credits allowances. Pro is $10/mo, Pro+ $39/mo, Max $100/mo, Business $19/seat/mo, Enterprise $39/seat/mo; usage beyond included credits is billed under usage-based billing, while legacy premium-request billing remains only for qualifying annual Pro/Pro+ subscribers.

name: Goose

form_factor.comment: Ships as a single Rust binary CLI plus a separate Desktop app (macOS/Linux/Windows) and an API. Per-form-factor links: CLI: https://goose-docs.ai/docs/getting-started/installation; Desktop App: https://goose-docs.ai/docs/getting-started/installation.

released_in.comment: Block's Open Source Program Office publicly announced "codename goose" on January 28, 2025, after ~9 months of internal development.

latest_major_update.comment: Latest AAIF Goose release v1.40.0 (July 2, 2026) adds desktop localization, CLI /status, /model and /goal commands, local goose review, async-task peek mode, delegate context/working_dir overrides, GLM-5.2 and many declarative providers, MLX local inference, streamable HTTP deeplink support, global ~/.agents/AGENTS.md hints, and unified thinking effort.

rules.comment: Goose looks for AGENTS.md then .goosehints by default, at both global (~/.config/goose/.goosehints) and per-directory/nested project scope; filename configurable via CONTEXT_FILE_NAMES env var. Location: ~/.config/goose/.goosehints (global), ./.goosehints or ./AGENTS.md (project, nested lookup).

skills.comment: Recipes are reusable, parameterized YAML workflow files (instructions, extensions, params, sub-recipes) that can be bound to custom slash commands, shared, and scheduled via cron. `/skills` slash command also lists available skills in-session. Location: recipe YAML files, e.g. ~/.config/goose/recipes/ or project-local paths passed via --recipe.

hooks.comment: Lifecycle hooks run shell scripts on events: SessionStart, SessionEnd, Stop, UserPromptSubmit, PreToolUse, PostToolUse, PostToolUseFailure, BeforeReadFile, AfterFileEdit, BeforeShellExecution, AfterShellExecution. Configured via hooks.json in plugin dirs. Location: ~/.agents/plugins/<name>/hooks/hooks.json (user scope) or <project>/.agents/plugins/<name>/hooks/hooks.json (project scope).

mcp_servers.comment: Goose's extension system is built natively on MCP; connects to 70+ documented extensions (GitHub, Docker, Postgres, Slack, Google Drive, browser automation, etc.) and any MCP-compatible server. Configurable via `goose configure`, `/extension`, `--with-extension`, `--with-streamable-http-extension`, or config.yaml.

custom_commands.comment: Recipes can be turned into custom slash commands (e.g. /weekly-status) usable in CLI or Desktop sessions. Location: recipe files referenced from goose config.

goal_command.comment: Goose v1.40.0 adds a built-in `/goal` command for agent self-evaluation before finishing.

subagents.comment: First-class subagent delegation: temporary AI instances spun up via natural language in a prompt for one-off tasks, running in isolation from the main session. Distinct from sub-recipes (pre-written reusable YAML workflows). Up to 10 concurrent parallel workers total across subagents/sub-recipes. Location: invoked ad hoc; sub-recipes configured via --sub-recipe / recipe YAML.

monitor.comment: Goose can delegate to subagents and run hooks around lifecycle/tool events, but reviewed docs do not show a dedicated background command/log monitor primitive.

transcripts.comment: Sessions stored in SQLite (v1.10.0+) at ~/.local/share/goose/sessions/sessions.db (Unix) or %APPDATA%\Block\goose\data\sessions\sessions.db (Windows); pre-v1.10.0 used per-session .jsonl files under ~/.local/share/goose/sessions/. `session export` can output markdown/json/yaml; `session list`/`session diagnostics` also available.

model_selection.comment: Full model/provider selection via `goose configure`, `--provider`/`--model` flags on `goose run`, or GOOSE_PROVIDER/GOOSE_MODEL env vars (e.g. export GOOSE_MODEL=claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929).

approval_mode.comment: Four permission modes: auto (full automation), approve (ask every tool unless allow/deny-listed), chat (no tool access), smart_approve (LLM-based PermissionJudge auto-approves safe calls, asks for risky ones). Set via `goose config set-mode <mode>`, the GOOSE_MODE env var, or in-session `/mode <name>`. No dedicated `--mode` CLI flag on `goose run`/`goose session`.

sandbox_mode.comment: `--container <id>` flag (on `goose session` and `goose run`) runs extensions inside a specified Docker container; goose CLI must be installed inside the container for built-in extensions to work.

resume.comment: `-r`/`--resume` flag on both `goose session` (resume previous chat) and `goose run` (resume from a previous run); also `--fork` to duplicate a session's history into a new one.

continue.comment: `--resume` without a session id/name resumes the most recent session by default, giving an effective "continue latest" path, but there is no separate dedicated `--continue` flag distinct from `--resume`.

non_interactive.comment: `goose run -t "..."` (or -i file) executes headlessly and exits; `--quiet`/`-q` suppresses non-response output for scripting; `--no-session` skips session file creation entirely. Designed for automation/CI pipelines.

output_format.comment: `--output-format <text|json|stream-json>` on `goose run` for machine-readable output; json gives final result, stream-json emits events as they occur. `session list`/`export` also support text/json/markdown/yaml.

statusline.comment: No documented user-configurable status line. CLI has theme and prompt-editor configuration but no status bar concept.

telemetry.comment: Anonymous usage data is opt-in/opt-out configurable interactively via `goose configure` or via GOOSE_TELEMETRY_ENABLED=false/true env var. No central control plane or phone-home by default per project description.

keyboard_shortcuts.comment: Some built-in shortcuts (Ctrl+C, Cmd+Up/Down history, Ctrl+R search) with limited customization, e.g. GOOSE_CLI_NEWLINE_KEY for the newline key. Not a fully remappable keybinding system.

theme_customization.comment: Theme set via `/t` slash command (light, dark, ansi) or GOOSE_CLI_THEME/GOOSE_CLI_LIGHT_THEME/GOOSE_CLI_DARK_THEME env vars for syntax highlighting.

auto_save.comment: Sessions are automatically persisted to the local SQLite session store as the conversation progresses; `--no-session` can disable this for one-off runs.

debugging_output.comment: `--debug` flag on `goose run`/`goose session` outputs complete tool responses, detailed parameter values, and full file paths. `goose session diagnostics` exports a debug bundle.

context_window.comment: Smart Context Management auto-compacts/summarizes older conversation turns at 80% of the model's token limit by default; GOOSE_CONTEXT_LIMIT overrides the displayed limit and GOOSE_AUTO_COMPACT_THRESHOLD controls the compaction trigger ratio.

code_formatting.comment: No built-in formatter; auto-run linters/formatters/tests can be wired via PostToolUse/AfterFileEdit lifecycle hooks or MCP developer/shell extensions rather than a native formatting feature.

git_integration.comment: Built-in Developer extension can run git directly via shell; GitHub MCP extension handles cloning, branching, editing, and committing changes end-to-end.

performance_mode.comment: Smart context compaction and `--max-tool-repetitions`/`--max-turns` limits act as performance/runaway-loop controls; no separate dedicated performance-tuning mode.

ui_customization.comment: CLI offers theme switching and a configurable external prompt editor (GOOSE_PROMPT_EDITOR); Desktop app has its own UI but no documented panel/layout rearrangement.

hosted_agent.comment: Runs locally as CLI/Desktop/API with no central control plane; no Goose-hosted cloud agent platform. `goose acp` exposes it as an Agent Client Protocol server for editor integration, not a hosted service.

custom_model_provider.comment: Supports 15+ LLM providers (Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, Azure, Bedrock, Databricks, Groq, OpenRouter, local Ollama, and more) including OpenAI-compatible custom endpoints, configured via `goose configure` or GOOSE_PROVIDER/GOOSE_MODEL plus provider API key env vars.

notes: Goose is an open-source (Apache-2.0), Rust-based agentic CLI and Desktop app originally built by Block and now stewarded by the Linux Foundation's Agentic AI Foundation (AAIF). It was the first open-source agent to support MCP and ships 70+ first-party MCP extensions, recipes/sub-recipes for reusable workflows, real subagent delegation, four-tier permission modes (auto/approve/chat/smart_approve via GOOSE_MODE), Docker-based execution sandboxing, and a recently added lifecycle-hooks plugin system. No paid tier; cost is purely BYOK LLM usage, with a fully free/no-account path via local Ollama models.

pricing.comment: Goose itself is free and open source under Apache-2.0 with no subscription or usage cap; users pay only for whichever LLM provider API they connect (or use free local Ollama models).

some_free_usage.comment: Google Gemini's free tier and free local Ollama models give a documented no-cost usage path alongside paid providers.

no_account_required.comment: Can run entirely against local Ollama models with no Goose account and no cloud provider account required.

can_use_other_subscriptions.comment: Routes agent usage through the user's own provider account/API key (Anthropic, OpenAI, Azure, Bedrock, Databricks, Groq, OpenRouter, etc.) or local inference (Ollama) rather than a Goose-owned subscription.

name: Grok Build

form_factor.comment: Terminal-native coding agent and CLI in early beta. Per-form-factor links: CLI: https://x.ai/news/grok-build-cli.

released_in.comment: xAI launch announcement for the early beta.

latest_major_update.comment: June 2026 updates added `/goal` for long-running autonomous execution, a plugin marketplace, an agent dashboard for parallel sessions, and Composer 2.5 model access in Grok Build.

rules.comment: Official launch page says AGENTS.md works out of the box. Location: `AGENTS.md` in repo tree.

skills.comment: Skills and plugin marketplace are called out in the launch page. Location: plugin/skill folders managed by Grok Build plugin marketplace; exact disk root not yet public.

hooks.comment: Hooks are explicitly listed as supported.

mcp_servers.comment: MCP servers work out of the box according to xAI. Location: MCP server configuration picked up from the repo/tooling; exact disk root not yet public.

custom_commands.comment: Plugin marketplace can bundle slash commands, but public docs reviewed still do not document a user-editable custom command folder.
