name: OpenHands

form_factor.comment: Local terminal CLI (`openhands`), browser terminal UI (`openhands web`), full Docker-based GUI server (`openhands serve`), OpenHands Cloud hosted agent (`openhands cloud`/`openhands login`), and IDE integration via Agent Client Protocol (`openhands acp`) for Zed/VS Code/JetBrains. Per-form-factor links: CLI: https://docs.openhands.dev/openhands/usage/cli/installation; Web: https://docs.openhands.dev/openhands/usage/cli/web-interface; Docker: https://docs.openhands.dev/openhands/usage/sandboxes/docker; Hosted Agent: https://app.all-hands.dev/; IDE: https://docs.openhands.dev/openhands/usage/cli/ide/overview.

released_in.comment: Launched March 12, 2024 as 'OpenDevin' (replicating Devin). First tagged GitHub release v0.6.1 on 2024-06-04 (earlier 0.1-0.5 history predates the public releases page). Renamed to OpenHands in mid-2024, and the GitHub org All-Hands-AI was renamed to OpenHands on 2025-10-20.

latest_major_update.comment: Latest public OpenHands release is cloud-1.40.0 (June 26, 2026), adding a full Git history user setting plus follow-up cloud fixes. The local/self-hosted 1.8.0 release remains the latest non-cloud semver release.

rules.comment: Root-level AGENTS.md (also supports CLAUDE.md/GEMINI.md variants) is injected into the system prompt at conversation start as always-on context. Location: repo root `AGENTS.md`.

skills.comment: Supports the AgentSkills standard (SKILL.md with progressive disclosure) plus a legacy simple format. Community registry at github.com/OpenHands/extensions. Older 'microagents' (.openhands/microagents/) are deprecated in favor of .agents/skills/. Location: `.agents/skills/<name>/SKILL.md` (recommended) or `.agents/skills/*.md` (legacy).

hooks.comment: Full lifecycle hooks (PreToolUse, PostToolUse, UserPromptSubmit, Stop, SessionStart, SessionEnd) configured via `.openhands/hooks.json`, format explicitly compatible with Claude Code hooks. Works across Cloud, CLI, and local GUI. Location: `.openhands/hooks.json` + `.openhands/hooks/*.sh`.

mcp_servers.comment: Full MCP support with stdio, SSE, and HTTP transports, OAuth/header auth, managed via `openhands mcp add/list/get/remove/enable/disable` CLI subcommands or by editing `~/.openhands/mcp.json` directly. `/mcp` shows live status in-session.

custom_commands.comment: Fixed set of built-in interactive `/` commands (/help, /new, /history, /confirm, /condense, /skills, /feedback, /exit). No documented mechanism for users to define their own custom slash commands (skills serve a similar reusable-prompt purpose instead).

goal_command.comment: No built-in `/goal` command for a persistent auto-continuing session objective; `/condense` manages context but does not implement a goal-loop.

subagents.comment: No documented first-class delegated/spawned subagent system for the CLI (contrast with Devin's 'Managed Devins'); OpenHands runs a single agent loop per conversation.

monitor.comment: No first-class delegated subagent or Monitor-style background watch primitive found in reviewed CLI docs; headless mode runs a task rather than monitoring arbitrary background jobs.

transcripts.comment: Conversation history automatically saved locally. Location: `~/.openhands/conversations/`. `--resume` lists and reopens prior conversations.

model_selection.comment: Set via Settings UI (Ctrl+P > Settings), persisted in `~/.openhands/agent_settings.json` (`llm.model` field), or temporarily overridden with `LLM_MODEL`/`LLM_API_KEY`/`LLM_BASE_URL` env vars plus the `--override-with-envs` flag.

approval_mode.comment: Confirmation enabled by default with LLM Risk Analyzer (auto-confirms low/medium risk, prompts on high risk). CLI flags `--always-approve` (auto-approve everything) and `--llm-approve` (force LLM-based analyzer) override behavior; `/confirm` adjusts in-session.

sandbox_mode.comment: Three sandbox providers: Docker (isolated container, default/recommended for `openhands serve`), Process (no isolation, runs directly as a local process — this is what the plain `openhands` CLI uses by default), and Remote (managed/hosted). Selected via legacy `RUNTIME` env var (docker/process/remote).

resume.comment: `--resume [ID]` resumes a specific conversation by ID, or lists recent conversations to pick from if no ID given.

continue.comment: `--resume --last` resumes the most recent conversation without needing to specify an ID.

non_interactive.comment: `openhands --headless -t "task"` (or `-f file`) runs with no UI for CI/CD and scripting. Always runs in always-approve mode in headless (cannot use --llm-approve here).

output_format.comment: `--json` flag (requires `--headless`) streams structured JSONL events (action/observation objects) for machine parsing in CI pipelines.

statusline.comment: Has 'live status monitoring' of agent progress in the TUI but no user-configurable status line / status bar string feature documented.

telemetry.comment: `/feedback` sends anonymous feedback about the CLI; no documented granular opt-in/opt-out telemetry settings page found beyond this and general OSS analytics conventions.

keyboard_shortcuts.comment: Fixed shortcuts (Ctrl+P command palette, Esc pause, Ctrl+Q/`/exit` quit, Ctrl+\ alt palette) viewable via palette 'Keys' option; no documented user remapping/customization.

theme_customization.comment: Command palette has a 'Theme' option to toggle color theme; no evidence of fully custom user-authored themes beyond toggling presets.

auto_save.comment: Conversation/session state and settings are auto-saved locally with no manual save step; file edits are written directly to disk by the agent as it works.

debugging_output.comment: `openhands web --debug` enables debug mode; `--json`/headless mode exposes structured event logs for diagnostics; broader DEBUG-related env vars exist in the wider config surface.

context_window.comment: `/condense` manually condenses conversation history to manage context; model-dependent context length is not separately user-configurable as a token-limit setting in the CLI.

code_formatting.comment: No built-in formatter/linter integration; users wire their own lint/format checks via `.openhands/setup.sh` or Stop hooks (e.g. `.openhands/hooks/lint_check.sh`) rather than a native formatting feature.

git_integration.comment: Agent runs git commands directly in the sandbox/process (clone, commit, branch, PR creation via GitHub integration); hooks can inject git status into prompts. GitHub Action integration also available (`run-openhands/github-action`).

performance_mode.comment: No documented user-facing caching/performance-tuning toggle in the CLI flag/config reference.

ui_customization.comment: Terminal UI is fixed layout; no panel/sidebar/layout customization documented beyond theme toggle.

hosted_agent.comment: OpenHands Cloud (app.all-hands.dev) is a fully managed hosted agent platform; `openhands login`/`openhands cloud -t "task"` create and run cloud-hosted conversations from the local CLI.

custom_model_provider.comment: LLM_BASE_URL plus LiteLLM backend allow pointing at any OpenAI-compatible endpoint, including local model servers (Ollama, vLLM, SGLang) and proxies.

notes: OpenHands launched in March 2024 as 'OpenDevin' (an open clone of Cognition's Devin), renamed OpenHands mid-2024, and had its GitHub org renamed All-Hands-AI -> OpenHands on 2025-10-20. The 1.0.0 release (Dec 2025) was a ground-up rewrite onto a new 'software-agent-sdk', changing config from TOML to JSON and rebranding 'microagents' to the AgentSkills-standard 'Skills' system. It has a genuine local CLI (`openhands`, installable via `uv tool install openhands` or `pip install openhands`, MIT licensed) that runs as a plain local process by default — Docker is only required for the separate `openhands serve` full-GUI mode, not the terminal CLI/headless mode.

pricing.value: Free (MIT, self-hosted); OpenHands Cloud free tier + at-cost usage; Enterprise custom
pricing.comment: Self-hosted/local CLI is free and open source (MIT license) other than your own LLM API costs. OpenHands Cloud Individual tier is free to sign up with a cap (10 max daily conversations) and offers at-cost LLM usage with no markup if you don't bring your own key. Enterprise (SaaS or self-hosted VPC) is custom-priced.

some_free_usage.comment: OpenHands Cloud Individual tier is free to use (capped at 10 daily conversations) and self-hosted CLI is entirely free software; cost is only the LLM provider's own usage.

no_account_required.comment: Local CLI installed via uv/pip/binary runs fully on your machine; first-run setup just configures your own LLM provider/API key in ~/.openhands/settings.json, no OpenHands account needed unless using `openhands login`/OpenHands Cloud.

can_use_other_subscriptions.comment: Connects to any LiteLLM-supported provider (Anthropic, OpenAI, OpenRouter, local Ollama/vLLM/SGLang, etc.) via your own API key, configured through Settings UI, ~/.openhands/agent_settings.json, or LLM_API_KEY/LLM_MODEL/LLM_BASE_URL env vars with --override-with-envs.

name: Pi

form_factor.comment: Terminal CLI via npm or curl installer and SDK docs for embedding. Per-form-factor links: CLI: https://pi.dev/docs/latest/quickstart and README install section; SDK: https://pi.dev/docs/latest/sdk.

released_in.comment: Pi changelog shows first listed public release as 0.74.0 dated 2026-05-07.

latest_major_update.comment: Latest release v0.80.3 (June 30, 2026) adds Anthropic Claude Sonnet 5 support through inherited Anthropic-compatible and Bedrock provider catalogs with adaptive thinking enabled.

rules.comment: Loads `AGENTS.md` and `CLAUDE.md` from `~/.pi/agent/`, ancestors, and cwd. Supports global and project context files with path-based precedence.

skills.comment: Discovers skills from `~/.pi/agent/skills/`, `~/.agents/skills/`, `.pi/skills/`, and `.agents/skills/`, including `SKILL.md` directories. Supports `/skill:name` command registration and cross-harness imports (e.g. Claude/ Codex).

hooks.comment: Extension API supports lifecycle/tool hooks via TypeScript extensions, including tool event hooks and custom event handlers. Full extension-based hook model from docs and examples.

mcp_servers.comment: README explicitly states no MCP. MCP-like capabilities can be built via extensions instead.

custom_commands.comment: Rich slash-command UI includes built-ins like `/model`, `/resume`, `/tree`, `/fork`, `/clone`, `/compact`, `/export`, `/share`, `/settings`, and `/login`.

goal_command.comment: No `/goal` concept is documented; README is explicit about what is *not* built in (including plan mode and no-permission prompts).

subagents.comment: README says "No sub-agents." suggests using spawned pi instances/context tools instead of native subagent orchestration.

monitor.comment: Pi docs explicitly say there are no native subagents, and reviewed docs do not show a Monitor-style background watch primitive.

model_selection.comment: Model/provider selection is fully exposed with `/model` in interactive mode and `--provider`, `--model`, `--models` CLI options.

approval_mode.comment: Philosophy page says there are no permission popups by default and no approval mode is documented as a first-class control.

sandbox_mode.comment: No sandbox mode setting is documented; README points users to tmux/container-like patterns instead of built-in sandbox controls.

resume.comment: Sessions can be resumed with `pi -r/--resume`, `--session`, and interactive `/resume` + `/fork`/`/clone` branch flow.

continue.comment: Continue latest session with `-c/--continue`; also supports session browsing and tree-based continuation commands in-session.

non_interactive.comment: Supports print mode (`-p/--print`) and machine-friendly transports (`--mode json`, `--mode rpc`) for scripted/CI usage.

output_format.comment: `--mode json` outputs JSON lines and JSON mode docs exist for event streaming consumers and integrations.

statusline.comment: Interactive UI includes a built-in footer/status area but no documented customization API for a user-defined status line.

telemetry.comment: Telemetry is configurable via `enableInstallTelemetry` and can be disabled; `PI_SKIP_VERSION_CHECK` and offline modes control network checks.

custom_model_provider.comment: Supports custom provider/API integration through `~/.pi/agent/models.json` and custom provider registration hooks for OpenAI-compatible, Anthropic, Google, and custom flows.

notes: Pi is a minimal terminal coding harness with extensibility-first defaults (extensions, skills, prompt templates, themes, packages), plus SDK and RPC/event-stream integrations.

pricing.comment: Repository is MIT-licensed and distributed as npm package `@earendil-works/pi-coding-agent`; there is no separate paid subscription model documented in public docs.

no_account_required.comment: Pi supports custom providers/models including Ollama, vLLM, LM Studio, and proxies, so local usage need not require a Pi-hosted account.

can_use_other_subscriptions.comment: Pi documents subscription-based providers via OAuth plus API-key providers and auth-file/env configuration.

hosted_agent.comment: CLI is local-first; no dedicated hosted runtime platform is documented.

transcripts.comment: Sessions are saved automatically to `~/.pi/agent/sessions/` with session IDs and `/session` command showing file and metadata.

name: Qwen Code

form_factor.comment: CLI + VS Code/Zed/JetBrains IDE integrations + official Qwen Code Desktop + daemon mode + SDKs + IM bots/channels. Per-form-factor links: CLI: https://qwenlm.github.io/qwen-code-docs/en/users/quickstart/; Extension: https://qwenlm.github.io/qwen-code-docs/en/users/extension/introduction/; SDK: https://qwenlm-qwen-code.mintlify.app/sdk/quick-start; Desktop App: https://github.com/QwenLM/qwen-code#how-to-use-qwen-code.

released_in.comment: GitHub repo created 2025-06-26. Earliest releases (v0.0.5+) shortly after. Current latest release: v0.19.2 (2026-06-24).

latest_major_update.comment: Latest release v0.19.4 (July 1, 2026) adds configurable auto-compact threshold and Stop-hook context usage, resumes interrupted turns without synthetic continue messages, adds desktop voice dictation, revives the Chrome extension through daemon-direct architecture, improves loop task injection, starts channel-agent bridge work, and fixes CLI/core/web-shell/standalone issues.

rules.comment: Hierarchical context files (QWEN.md) at project/user levels. Custom system prompts via --system-prompt and --append-system-prompt. Layered config across system/user/project/env/cli. Location: Qwen Code project/user instruction files and extension packages; commonly `AGENTS.md`-style repo context plus Qwen config.

skills.comment: Full Agent Skills system: SKILL.md with YAML frontmatter, model-invoked, personal (~/.qwen/skills/) and project (.qwen/skills/), path gating, extension skills. Location: Qwen Code extensions package skills via `qwen-extension.json`.

hooks.comment: 14 hook events: PreToolUse, PostToolUse, SessionStart, SessionEnd, SubagentStart/Stop, PreCompact, PostCompact, Notification, PermissionRequest. Three executors: command, http, function.

mcp_servers.comment: Full MCP: stdio, SSE, HTTP transports. OAuth 2.0, tool filtering, server trust, global allow/deny lists. CLI management via qwen mcp commands. Location: Qwen Code MCP settings in Qwen config/extension packages.

custom_commands.comment: Full custom commands: Markdown files in ~/.qwen/commands/ (global) and .qwen/commands/ (project). Support {{args}}, !{shell}, @{file} references. Namespace support. Location: Qwen Code extensions can package custom commands via `qwen-extension.json`.

goal_command.comment: Built-in `/goal` sets a goal and keeps working until the condition is met; supports `/goal <condition>` and `/goal clear`.

subagents.comment: Full subagent system: named subagents with YAML frontmatter, fork subagents (context-inheriting), per-agent model/permission/tool config, extension subagents.

monitor.comment: Qwen Code has subagents, hooks, headless mode, and status surfaces, but reviewed docs do not show a dedicated Monitor-style arbitrary command/log/WebSocket watcher.

model_selection.comment: Full model switching via /model. Supports OpenAI-compatible, Anthropic, Gemini protocols. Local models via Ollama/vLLM. Per-model reasoning config.

approval_mode.comment: Four modes: Plan (read-only), Default (manual approval), Auto-Edit (auto file edits, manual shell), YOLO (auto all). Shift+Tab to cycle.

sandbox_mode.comment: Full sandboxing: macOS Seatbelt (sandbox-exec with 6 profiles) and Docker/Podman containers (full isolation, custom images, network proxying, UID/GID mapping).

resume.comment: /resume command in interactive mode. --resume <sessionId> in headless mode. Session data as JSONL under ~/.qwen/projects/.

continue.comment: --continue flag resumes the most recent session for the current project in headless mode.

non_interactive.comment: -p/--prompt for headless. stdin piping, JSON output, resume/continue, persistent retry mode for CI. GitHub Actions integration.

output_format.comment: Three formats: text (default), json (structured array), stream-json (line-delimited, real-time). --include-partial-messages for granular streaming.

statusline.comment: Custom status line via ui.statusLine in settings.json. Shell command receives JSON context. Multi-line support, hot reload, refreshInterval for timer-based updates.

telemetry.comment: OpenTelemetry-based: traces, logs, metrics. Export to local file, OTLP collector, Alibaba Cloud Managed Service. Privacy opt-out via privacy.usageStatisticsEnabled.

custom_model_provider.comment: modelProviders config with openai/anthropic/gemini auth types. Local models via any OpenAI-compatible endpoint (Ollama, vLLM, LM Studio). OpenRouter, Fireworks AI.

notes: Qwen Code by Alibaba (QwenLM) is the most feature-complete open-source coding agent in this matrix -- 20/20 fields at full (tied with Cline). Standout features: macOS Seatbelt + Docker sandbox, OpenTelemetry telemetry, custom status line, three output formats. Apache-2.0, 24.6k stars.

pricing.comment: Tool is free and open-source (Apache 2.0). Model costs: BYO API key (provider pricing), Alibaba Cloud Coding Plan (fixed monthly), or local models (free).

hosted_agent.comment: Can use Alibaba Cloud Coding Plan for hosted model access; agent runs locally.

name: Roo Code

form_factor.comment: VS Code extension + CLI. Per-form-factor links: Extension: https://docs.roocode.com/getting-started/installing.

released_in.comment: Forked from Cline. Earliest releases ~mid-2025. Reached v3.54.0 by shutdown on May 15, 2026.

latest_major_update.comment: Final release v3.54.0 on May 15, 2026 (shutdown/sunsetting release). Repository archived, read-only.

rules.comment: Comprehensive: .roorules, .roo/rules/, .clinerules fallback, ~/.roo/rules/ global dir, AGENTS.md/AGENT.md support, mode-specific rules via rules-{modeSlug}. Location: no public disk folder/file location found in docs reviewed.

skills.comment: Full skills (added v3.38.0, Dec 2025). Follows agentskills.io spec: SKILL.md with frontmatter, progressive discovery, mode-specific targeting, bundled resources. Location: no public disk folder/file location found in docs reviewed.

hooks.comment: No lifecycle hooks system found in any documentation. Built-in slash commands exist but no user-defined lifecycle hooks.

mcp_servers.comment: Full MCP since v2.2.0. STDIO, Streamable HTTP, SSE transports. Roo Code Marketplace for one-click MCP install. Per-tool alwaysAllow permissions. Location: no public disk folder/file location found in docs reviewed.

custom_commands.comment: Full slash commands: .roo/commands/ (project) and ~/.roo/commands/ (global). Frontmatter with description, argument-hint, mode targeting. Built-in /init command. Location: no public disk folder/file location found in docs reviewed.

goal_command.comment: Roo Code supports custom slash commands; no built-in `/goal` for persistent session objectives.

subagents.comment: Full orchestration via Orchestrator Mode (Boomerang Mode). new_task tool delegates to specialized modes. Isolated context with explicit context transfer.

monitor.comment: Roo Code can delegate work through Orchestrator/Boomerang mode, but reviewed docs do not show a dedicated background command/log/WebSocket monitor primitive.

model_selection.comment: 22+ documented providers: Anthropic, OpenAI, AWS Bedrock, DeepSeek, Google Gemini, LM Studio, Ollama, OpenRouter, and more. Sticky models per mode.

approval_mode.comment: Granular auto-approval: read/write operations, approved commands (allowlist/denylist), MCP tools (dual permission), mode switching, subtasks, follow-up questions.

sandbox_mode.comment: No explicit sandbox mode. Checkpoints provide git-based recovery. .rooignore for access control. No isolated/sandboxed execution environment.

resume.comment: Checkpoints provide 'Restore Files & Task'. CLI has --create-with-session-id and session resume/history support (v3.51.0+).

continue.comment: CLI session resume/history support. Per-task history persists and can be resumed. History view shows past tasks for reopening.

non_interactive.comment: CLI binary (roo) with headless mode. NDJSON stdin/stdout stream protocol. Subcommands: run, list, upgrade. Platform binaries available.

output_format.comment: CLI supports NDJSON stdin stream protocol for structured I/O. exitCode in tool_result events. No formal --json or --print flag.

statusline.comment: No custom status line feature found. VS Code extension has chat panel UI but no configurable status line.

telemetry.comment: PostHog-powered. Anonymous feature usage, VS Code machine ID, error reports. No PII/code/prompts. Opt-out available. API error telemetry per provider.

custom_model_provider.comment: OpenAI Compatible provider for arbitrary API endpoints. LiteLLM routing. 22+ built-in providers. Debug proxy support. Model-agnostic.

notes: Roo Code was a popular Cline fork (24k stars, 574k VS Code installs) that was shut down on May 15, 2026. The repository is now archived and read-only. VS Code extension-host API only; no standalone npm agent SDK. Community forks include ZooCode and Cline.

pricing.comment: Free and open-source (Apache 2.0). Users paid their own LLM provider API costs. Roo Code Router was a paid routing service. Now discontinued.

hosted_agent.comment: Runs locally as VS Code extension or CLI. Project is now archived.

transcripts.comment: Location: transcript/session disk storage was not found in public docs reviewed.

keyboard_shortcuts.comment: VS Code keybindings support.

theme_customization.comment: VS Code theme inheritance.

auto_save.comment: Workspace auto-save configurable.

debugging_output.comment: Debug output and logging available.

context_window.comment: Context length configurable.

code_formatting.comment: VS Code formatter integration.

git_integration.comment: Full VS Code git support.

name: Trae Agent

form_factor.comment: Research-friendly CLI coding agent. Per-form-factor links: CLI: https://github.com/bytedance/trae-agent#usage.

released_in.comment: GitHub repository created June 13, 2025; no published GitHub releases as of this run.

latest_major_update.comment: No GitHub releases are published. Current main README describes active development, Python 3.12+, CLI task execution, interactive mode, trajectory recording, YAML config, MCP services, Docker execution flags, and multi-provider support.

rules.comment: YAML configuration can define agent model, max steps, and tool set, but no AGENTS.md-style repository instruction file is documented.

skills.comment: No reusable skill system documented; roadmap focuses on SDK, sandbox, trajectory analysis, MCP, and multi-agent support.

hooks.comment: No lifecycle hooks documented.

mcp_servers.comment: README documents optional `mcp_servers` configuration; roadmap says MCP/tool ecosystem expansion is still planned.

custom_commands.comment: Only built-in CLI commands (`run`, `interactive`, `show-config`, `tools`) are documented.

goal_command.comment: No persistent `/goal` or auto-continue-until-complete command documented.

subagents.comment: Multi-agent support is listed on the roadmap, not documented as current functionality.

monitor.comment: Multi-agent support is still roadmap-only and reviewed CLI docs do not show a Monitor-style background watch primitive.

model_selection.comment: Supports `--provider`, `--model`, `--model-base-url`, and config-file model providers.

approval_mode.comment: No tool approval/autonomy mode flags documented.

sandbox_mode.comment: `trae-cli run` supports Docker image/container/Dockerfile flags, but no default local sandbox mode.

resume.comment: No resume/session ID command documented.

continue.comment: No continue-latest command documented.

non_interactive.comment: `trae-cli run` executes a single task and exits.

output_format.comment: No stdout JSON mode documented, but trajectory files capture LLM interactions, agent steps, tool usage, and metadata as JSON.

statusline.comment: No configurable statusline documented.

telemetry.comment: No product telemetry collection documented; trajectory recording is local/debug oriented.

custom_model_provider.comment: Supports OpenAI, Anthropic, Doubao, Azure, OpenRouter, Ollama, Google Gemini, custom base URLs, and config-file providers.

pricing.comment: Trae Agent is MIT-licensed. Users provide provider API keys or local Ollama models.

some_free_usage.comment: Local Ollama provider support enables account-free/free local model usage.

no_account_required.comment: Can run with local Ollama models without a hosted Trae account.

can_use_other_subscriptions.comment: Bring-your-own provider/API keys for multiple providers.

hosted_agent.comment: Local CLI agent; no hosted/cloud agent platform documented.

transcripts.comment: Trajectory recording is first-class, with auto-generated or custom trajectory JSON files.

keyboard_shortcuts.comment: Research needed. Public docs did not expose enough detail in this run.

theme_customization.comment: CLI/interactive interface has no documented theme customization.

auto_save.comment: Research needed. Public docs did not expose enough detail in this run.

debugging_output.comment: Trajectory files and `show-config` support debugging/analysis.

context_window.comment: Model config includes `max_tokens`, but no dedicated context-window management/compaction docs were found.

code_formatting.comment: Research needed. Public docs did not expose enough detail in this run.

git_integration.comment: Supports patch generation/application paths and file editing, but no first-class git commit/PR workflow documented.

performance_mode.comment: Research needed. Public docs did not expose enough detail in this run.

ui_customization.comment: CLI supports simple/rich console types; no broader UI customization documented.

notes: Official ByteDance open-source CLI agent for software engineering research and development. Verified locally from documented `uv sync --all-extras`: `trae-cli --help`, `trae-cli run --help`, and `trae-cli interactive --help` work. Added to agent-launch as `--agent trae` targeting `trae-cli`.

name: Windsurf Cascade

form_factor.comment: Devin Desktop/Windsurf IDE + JetBrains plugin + CLI launcher. The legacy `surf`/`windsurf` shell commands are retained for compatibility, while the current install command is `devin-desktop`. Per-form-factor links: IDE: https://devin.ai/download; Extension: https://docs.devin.ai/desktop/command/plugins-overview; CLI: https://docs.devin.ai/desktop/devin-desktop-faq.

released_in.comment: Originally launched as 'Codeium' IDE, rebranded to 'Windsurf' ~November 2024. Windsurf 2.0 shipped April 15, 2026.

latest_major_update.comment: Devin Desktop/Windsurf v3.3.18 (June 23, 2026) shows Devin ACU usage in-client, fixes settings/extensions migration for Windows system-wide installs, resolves Windows bash to Git Bash, adds default subagent models, warms the MCP registry cache, fixes ACP agent-message merging, and adds an attribution config option.

rules.comment: Three scopes: Global (~/.codeium/windsurf/memories/global_rules.md, always-on), Workspace (.windsurf/rules/*.md with frontmatter modes), System (/etc/windsurf/rules/*.md). AGENTS.md support. Location: no public disk folder/file location found in docs reviewed.

skills.comment: Full support. SKILL.md files with YAML frontmatter. Progressive disclosure. Scopes: Workspace .windsurf/skills/, Global ~/.codeium/windsurf/skills/. Discovers .agents/skills/ and .claude/skills/. Location: no public disk folder/file location found in docs reviewed.

hooks.comment: 12 hook events: pre/post read/write code, pre/post run command, pre/post MCP tool use, pre/post user prompt, post cascade response. Pre-hooks can block with exit code 2.

mcp_servers.comment: Full native MCP. MCP Marketplace with one-click install. stdio, Streamable HTTP, SSE transports. OAuth support. Admin whitelists for teams. Location: no public disk folder/file location found in docs reviewed.

custom_commands.comment: Custom commands via Workflows (slash commands /[workflow-name]) and Command palette. Terminal has 4 auto-execution levels. Location: no public disk folder/file location found in docs reviewed.

goal_command.comment: Windsurf Cascade has workflows and hooks but no documented `/goal` slash command for persistent objectives.

subagents.comment: Devin Cloud (autonomous agent on own VM), Devin Local Agent, Fast Context (specialized 20x faster retrieval), Agent Command Center manages all agents in Kanban view.

monitor.comment: Windsurf/Devin surfaces support cloud/local agent status, hooks, and agent command workflows, but reviewed docs do not show a dedicated arbitrary command/log/WebSocket monitor primitive.

model_selection.comment: Full selection: SWE-1.6/1.5, Claude Opus/Sonnet, GPT-5.x series, Gemini, DeepSeek V4, Kimi, Grok, and more. Adaptive (intelligent router) and Arena Mode (side-by-side).

approval_mode.comment: 4 terminal levels: Disabled (require approval), Allowlist Only, Auto (Cascade judges), Turbo (all auto-executed). Allow/deny lists. Cascade Code/Chat/Ask mode distinctions.

sandbox_mode.comment: Devin cloud agent runs on isolated VM. Git worktrees isolate parallel sessions. Dev Containers support. No standalone sandbox toggle for the IDE.

resume.comment: Cascade has 'continue' button. Auto-Continue setting. Named checkpoints/snapshots for reversion. Sessions persist and switch via dropdown.

continue.comment: Auto-Continue setting automatically continues when hitting tool-call limits. Queued messages for follow-ups while Cascade is working. Real-time editor awareness.

non_interactive.comment: `windsurf` command-line launcher can be installed into PATH from the IDE/desktop app. Devin CLI agent runs in terminal. No documented --non-interactive flag for the Windsurf IDE launcher.

output_format.comment: Enterprise analytics API available. post_cascade_response_with_transcript hook writes full conversation to JSONL files at ~/.windsurf/transcripts/{trajectory_id}.jsonl.

statusline.comment: No customizable status line like Cursor. Status bar has model indicator and plan/usage info. Agent Command Center is a Kanban dashboard of all agents.

telemetry.comment: Telemetry settings mentioned in troubleshooting docs. Enterprise analytics API. No documented granular telemetry opt-out beyond standard VS Code settings.

custom_model_provider.comment: BYOK for Anthropic models (Claude Sonnet 4, Opus 4) for Free/Pro users. No generic custom OpenAI-compatible endpoint configuration documented.

notes: Windsurf by Codeium (acquired by Cognition AI in April 2026) is a standalone VS Code fork IDE with integrated coding agent (Cascade). Enterprise analytics API only; no agent embed SDK. Competes directly with Cursor IDE.

pricing.comment: Free ($0): Unlimited autocomplete, 50 Cascade requests weekly. Pro ($20/mo): Standard daily usage + extra at API prices. Max ($200/mo): Heavy daily usage. Teams ($40/user/mo).

hosted_agent.comment: Devin cloud agent integration provides managed agent platform.

transcripts.comment: Location: transcript/session disk storage was not found in public docs reviewed.

name: ZCode

form_factor.comment: Desktop agentic development environment for macOS and Windows, Linux beta group, plus mobile Remote Control for directing an open desktop workspace. Per-form-factor links: IDE: https://zcode.z.ai/en; Desktop App: https://zcode.z.ai/en/docs/install; Mobile: https://zcode.z.ai/en/docs/remote-control.

released_in.comment: Public ZCode docs and desktop downloads are live in 2026; exact public launch date not confirmed in accessible docs.

latest_major_update.comment: Latest public ZCode release v3.2.2 (July 1, 2026) adds built-in plugin update/uninstall management, file-rewind safety summaries, improved prompt command trigger suggestions, more accurate subagent/tool usage counts, clearer external-tool errors, stopped-goal status visibility, spacing/text fixes, and stronger sensitive-data redaction in log export.

rules.comment: Reads user global `~/.zcode/AGENTS.md` and workspace `AGENTS.md`; workspace instructions are primary and CLAUDE.md is only a one-time onboarding migration source.

skills.comment: First-class `SKILL.md` skills under `~/.zcode/skills/<skill-name>/SKILL.md`, with Settings management and import from other agents.

hooks.comment: Plugin system advertises Hook plugin types, but current public docs do not expose a full standalone hook API like some CLIs.

mcp_servers.comment: Supports stdio, HTTP, and SSE MCP servers, full JSON config import, env vars/headers, plugin MCP servers, and importing MCP config from Claude Code, Codex CLI, OpenCode, and `.agents`.

custom_commands.comment: User/workspace custom slash commands can be created in settings and invoked from `/` in ZCode Agent.

goal_command.comment: Built-in `/goal` supports set, replace, pause, resume, and clear; the agent automatically verifies each round and continues until the goal is complete.

subagents.comment: Built-in general-purpose and Explore subagents plus beta user-level custom subagents under `~/.zcode/agents/<name>.md`, with custom model/tool permissions/instructions.

monitor.comment: ZCode has built-in/custom subagents and workspace task surfaces, but reviewed docs do not show a dedicated command/log/WebSocket Monitor-equivalent.

model_selection.comment: Model selector and model settings support Z.ai, BigModel, GLM Coding Plan, and API-key providers.

approval_mode.comment: Execution modes include Default, Confirm Before Changes, and Auto Edit; safety confirmation pauses risky actions for approval.

sandbox_mode.comment: Remote development can run workspaces in SSH hosts or local Docker containers; no standalone local sandbox flag is documented.

resume.comment: Desktop task list/conversation history supports returning to prior work, but no CLI-style `--resume` flag is documented.

continue.comment: Existing tasks can be continued from desktop or mobile Remote Control; no headless continue command is documented.

non_interactive.comment: ZCode is a desktop ADE; no local headless CLI entry point was found.

output_format.comment: No JSON/structured output mode documented for the desktop agent.

statusline.comment: Goal summary panel shows status, elapsed time, tokens, and iteration count, but no custom statusline API is documented.

telemetry.comment: Usage Stats exists; no telemetry opt-out/control docs found.

custom_model_provider.comment: Supports third-party compatible model services through Anthropic/OpenAI protocols and API key mode.

pricing.value: 5-day free trial; GLM Coding Plan or API/provider billing
pricing.comment: First-time ZCode users get a 5-day free trial; GLM Coding Plan subscribers get limited-time ~1.5x quota in ZCode through July 31, 2026. Longer-term use depends on GLM Coding Plan/API/provider billing.

some_free_usage.comment: First-time ZCode users receive a 5-day free trial with daily GLM-5.2/GLM-5-Turbo token benefits.

hosted_agent.comment: Runtime remains the desktop workspace or remote SSH/Docker target; mobile remote only controls the open desktop workspace.

transcripts.comment: Task list/conversation history is first-class in the desktop UI, but public docs do not expose disk transcript paths.

keyboard_shortcuts.comment: Command Center is opened with Command+K; docs include keyboard shortcut support.

theme_customization.comment: Command Center includes switch-theme actions.

auto_save.comment: Research needed. Public docs did not expose enough detail in this run.

debugging_output.comment: Log export exists and was improved in v3.2.2; public docs do not expose a full debug/verbose mode.

context_window.comment: ZCode is built around GLM-5.2 long-context coding workflows and usage stats show context-window breakdown.

code_formatting.comment: Research needed. Public docs did not expose enough detail in this run.

git_integration.comment: ZCode Agent understands Git branch state and combines workspace state, tool results, and Git changes during tasks.

performance_mode.comment: Execution modes and model selector can trade off confirmation/autonomy and model choice, but no named performance mode is documented.

ui_customization.comment: Desktop UI supports interface scaling and theme switching; deeper layout customization is not documented.

notes: Z.ai/Zhipu desktop ADE with in-house ZCode Agent, GLM-5.2 focus, AGENTS.md, skills, commands, MCP, subagents, plugins, goal mode, mobile remote control, bot channels, and remote SSH/Docker development. No local CLI command was found, so no agent-launch backend was added.
